title: M3a: copyTemplate helper + provisioned marker
description:
**Goal:** Optional template seeding on workspace creation. File: `src/server/workspace/provision.ts` (~30 LOC).

**Shape:**
```ts
export async function copyTemplate(templatePath: string | undefined, workspaceRoot: string): Promise<void> {
  if (!templatePath) return
  const marker = path.join(workspaceRoot, '.boring-agent/provisioned')
  if (await fileExists(marker)) return   // idempotent
  await fs.cp(templatePath, workspaceRoot, { recursive: true, errorOnExist: false })
  await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(marker), {recursive:true})
  await fs.writeFile(marker, new Date().toISOString())
}
```

**Call site:** synchronously during workspace creation, after `mkdir(workspaceRoot)`. For expected template sizes (skill files, README, config or two) this is milliseconds.

**Configuration (priority order):**
1. `createAgentApp({ templatePath })` — primary API.
2. `BORING_AGENT_TEMPLATE_PATH` env — fallback.
3. Unset → no seeding.

**Remote mode:** in M2, template is packaged as a tarball and fed to `Sandbox.create({ source: { type: 'tarball', url } })`. Local mode uses fs.cp. (Remote path added in M2 or as M3a polish — annotate as TODO.)

**Tests:** copies template; second call no-ops (marker present); missing source throws clearly.

## Acceptance Criteria

Copies template on first creation. Second call no-ops (marker present). Missing source throws clearly. Remote mode tarball path works.
acceptance_criteria: Copies template on first creation. Second call no-ops (marker present). Missing source throws clearly. Remote mode tarball path works.

title: Phase 2: Code Editor Pane (CodeMirror 6)
description:
## Goal
Full CodeMirror 6 code editor — standalone component + dockview pane wrapper. NEW code (v1 uses react-simple-code-editor + Prism).

## What to implement

### CodeEditor (src/components/CodeEditor.tsx) — STANDALONE, Tier 3
- Props-only, zero context dependency (tenth pass)
- Props: content: string, onChange: (content: string) => void, language: string, readOnly?: boolean, lineNumbers?: boolean, wordWrap?: boolean, className?: string
- CodeMirror 6 integration (full rewrite, not a port)
- Language support: JS/TS, Python, JSON, YAML, Markdown, SQL (all bundled initially; measure chunk size — cut to 3 bundled + lazy rest only if total >800KB budget exceeded)
- Theme: wire CM6 theme to shadcn CSS variables via EditorView.theme()
- Line numbers (default on), word wrap toggle
- Large file handling:
  - <1MB: full syntax highlighting, incremental Lezer parsing
  - >=1MB: read-only mode, syntax highlighting disabled, banner: "Large file — editing disabled" + download link for >10MB

### CodeEditorPane (src/panes/CodeEditorPane.tsx) — dockview wrapper
- Reads file content from DataProvider (useFileContent hook)
- Writes via useFileWrite mutation
- Uses useEditorLifecycle hook for dirty tracking + auto-save
- Tab dirty indicator (Badge on tab)
- External file change detection: auto-sync when not dirty, suppress stale reads 3s after save
- Lazy-loaded (~250KB gz with CodeMirror)
- Single mode: normal edit (no diff modes — deferred to v1.x)

### codemirror-theme.ts (src/theme/codemirror-theme.ts)
- CM6 theme reading shadcn CSS variables at runtime
- Light + dark modes
- Zero CSS override needed (CM6 themed via API, not CSS)

Implementation approach:
```typescript
// theme/codemirror-theme.ts
import { EditorView } from '@codemirror/view'

export function createShadcnTheme() {
  return EditorView.theme({
    '&': {
      backgroundColor: 'var(--background)',
      color: 'var(--foreground)',
    },
    '.cm-gutters': {
      backgroundColor: 'var(--muted)',
      color: 'var(--muted-foreground)',
      borderRight: '1px solid var(--border)',
    },
    '.cm-activeLineGutter': {
      backgroundColor: 'var(--accent)',
    },
    '.cm-activeLine': {
      backgroundColor: 'var(--accent)',
    },
    '&.cm-focused .cm-cursor': {
      borderLeftColor: 'var(--foreground)',
    },
    '.cm-selectionBackground, &.cm-focused .cm-selectionBackground': {
      backgroundColor: 'var(--accent)',
    },
  })
}
```

### Risk 8: Editor keybinding isolation (dual editor conflict)
v2 ships both tiptap (markdown) and CodeMirror 6 (code). Both register keyboard handlers. Mitigation:

1. Only the focused editor captures keystrokes (both scope to their DOM container)
2. Dockview onDidActivePanelChange coordinates:
   - When panel activates, disable keyboard listeners on previously active editor
   - tiptap: editor.setEditable(false) on blur, true on focus
   - CodeMirror: EditorView.dispatch({ effects: readOnlyCompartment.reconfigure(...) }) on blur/focus
3. Workspace-level shortcuts (Cmd+P, Cmd+B sidebar) registered on workspace container, always win via event.stopPropagation() guard

Pseudo-code from plan:
```typescript
// hooks/useEditorFocusManagement.ts
function useEditorFocusManagement(api: DockviewApi) {
  useEffect(() => {
    const disposable = api.onDidActivePanelChange((event) => {
      const prevPanel = event.previous
      const nextPanel = event.panel

      // Deactivate previous editor
      if (prevPanel?.params?.editorRef) {
        const editor = prevPanel.params.editorRef
        if (editor.type === 'tiptap') editor.setEditable(false)
        if (editor.type === 'codemirror') {
          editor.dispatch({ effects: readOnlyCompartment.reconfigure(EditorState.readOnly.of(true)) })
        }
      }

      // Activate new editor
      if (nextPanel?.params?.editorRef) {
        const editor = nextPanel.params.editorRef
        if (editor.type === 'tiptap') editor.setEditable(true)
        if (editor.type === 'codemirror') {
          editor.dispatch({ effects: readOnlyCompartment.reconfigure(EditorState.readOnly.of(false)) })
          editor.focus()
        }
      }
    })
    return () => disposable.dispose()
  }, [api])
}
```

### CM6 Extensions (10 extensions for code editing)
Based on plan Phase 2.4 — these are the CM6 extensions to include:

1. @codemirror/language (Lezer incremental parsing)
2. @codemirror/lang-javascript (JS/TS support)
3. @codemirror/lang-python
4. @codemirror/lang-json
5. @codemirror/lang-yaml (if available, else custom)
6. @codemirror/lang-markdown
7. @codemirror/lang-sql
8. @codemirror/view (EditorView, lineNumbers, highlightActiveLine)
9. @codemirror/state (EditorState, compartments for dynamic config)
10. @codemirror/search (search/replace functionality)

Plus: @codemirror/autocomplete, @codemirror/lint as optional future extensions.

### Performance budgets
| Metric | Target | Measured by |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| First CodeMirror render | <500ms | Playwright timer |
| Chunk size (CM6 + languages) | ~250KB gz | vite build analysis |
| Total all-loaded ceiling share | Must fit within 800KB total workspace budget | |

## v1 references
- CodeEditor.jsx (176 lines, react-simple-code-editor + Prism) — FULL REWRITE
- EditorPanel.jsx (417 lines) — file load/save/dirty pattern (PORT the pattern)

## Considerations
- CM6 has NO worker-based tokenization — Lezer parses incrementally on main thread
- The 1MB cutoff is simple and effective for v1
- Editor keybinding conflict handled by useEditorFocusManagement (Risk 8 mitigation)
- Performance budget: first CM6 render <500ms
- CSP: style-src 'unsafe-inline' required by CodeMirror 6's style-mod library (no nonce support upstream)

## Unit tests (components/CodeEditor.test.tsx)

### Standalone CodeEditor
- Test: renders with content and language props
- Test: onChange fires when content changes
- Test: readOnly mode prevents editing
- Test: lineNumbers prop toggles gutter visibility
- Test: wordWrap prop toggles line wrapping
- Test: language switching changes syntax highlighting
- Test: all 6 languages render without errors (JS, TS, Python, JSON, YAML, SQL, Markdown)

### Large file handling
- Test: file <1MB → full editing with syntax highlighting
- Test: file >=1MB → read-only banner shown, editing disabled
- Test: file >10MB → download link shown

### Theme integration
- Test: CM6 theme reads shadcn CSS variables
- Test: theme changes on workspace theme toggle (light↔dark)

### CodeEditorPane
- Test: loads file content via useFileContent hook
- Test: saves via useFileWrite on auto-save trigger
- Test: dirty indicator shows when content modified
- Test: external file change auto-syncs when not dirty
- Test: stale read suppression: no auto-sync within 3s of save
- Test: lazy-loaded via React.lazy (Suspense boundary works)

### Keybinding isolation
- Test: inactive CM6 editor does not capture keystrokes
- Test: Cmd+P always triggers workspace command palette, not CM6

## Success criteria
- Renders code with syntax highlighting for all 6 languages
- Large files (>=1MB) show read-only banner
- Theme syncs with workspace light/dark mode
- Standalone CodeEditor works without WorkspaceProvider (props-only)
- CodeEditorPane loads/saves via DataProvider, tracks dirty state
- Auto-save fires after 1000ms debounce
- External file changes auto-sync when editor is clean

title: @boring/agent v1 package
description:
Build the v1 `@boring/agent` package at `/home/ubuntu/projects/boring-ui-v2/packages/agent/`. Full spec at `packages/agent/docs/plans/agent-package-spec.md` (1,723 lines).

**Goal:** Deliverable, pane-embeddable agent package that ships three execution modes (direct / local-bwrap / vercel-sandbox) behind one mental model (4 abstractions: Harness, Catalog, Workspace, Sandbox; 3 integration services: SessionStore, UiBridge, SandboxHandleStore). Same code runs as a standalone CLI (`npx @boring/agent`) OR embedded by `@boring/workspace` as a chat pane.

**Why this matters:** Current boring-ui monorepo has the agent tangled into app-specific layouts. V2 cleanly splits: agent = chat + tools + sandbox; workspace = layouts that consume agent; cloud (future) = multi-workspace. Once v2 ships, apps like boring-macro migrate by flipping env vars.

**Architectural invariants (locked):**
- No `node:fs` / `node:child_process` in routes/catalog/tools — only inside adapter impls.
- `Workspace` + `Sandbox` always paired via `RuntimeModeAdapter` (shared fs substrate).
- `Uint8Array` (not `Buffer`) in all shared contracts.
- `UiBridge.postCommand` is single dispatch source; chat `data-ui-command` parts are display-only.
- Stream resume is transport-owned (route-level ring buffer), not harness-owned.

**Net v1 LOC target:** ~2,500.

**Shipping order:** M0 scaffold → M1 local MVP → M2 remote mode → M3a core UX contracts → M3b chat polish → M4 plugins → M5 workspace extension hooks.

Children are M0–M5 epics + cross-cutting infrastructure epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 6 milestone epics complete. `npx @boring/agent` works. CLI + standalone + pane-embed all function. ~2,500 LOC delivered. All invariants enforced in CI.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 milestone epics complete. `npx @boring/agent` works. CLI + standalone + pane-embed all function. ~2,500 LOC delivered. All invariants enforced in CI.

title: Research: Verify pi built-in tools fully opt-out
description:
**Question:** Does `createAgentSession({ tools: [our...] })` fully replace pi's default tool list, or do some defaults sneak through?

**Why critical:** if pi's `bash` or `read` ships alongside ours, name collisions break LLM tool choice. Our sandbox guarantees leak.

**Investigation:**
1. Build a minimal harness with only a `hello` tool.
2. Drive a turn where the LLM would normally invoke `bash` (e.g., "run `ls`").
3. Observe the tool catalog pi reports to the LLM — assert it contains ONLY `hello`.
4. If any pi defaults present: file upstream bug + document workaround (tool name namespacing: `boring.bash` vs pi's `bash`).

**Artifact:** conformance test `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/__tests__/builtinOptout.test.ts` that fails loudly if pi regresses.

**Acceptance:** test passes; any pi version upgrade re-runs this check.

## Acceptance Criteria

Conformance test passes: pi tools list contains ONLY our tools. Runs on every pi upgrade.

title: M3a-Risk: Pi SessionEntry → UIMessage conformance
description:
**Risk class:** Requires real mitigation. Spec §Risks: "We map pi's native SessionEntry records to AI SDK UIMessage. If pi evolves or edge cases slip, session resume / replay breaks."

**Mitigation:** conformance test that exercises EVERY known pi event type.

**Approach:**
1. Capture a fixture stream of pi SessionEntry records covering: text-delta, tool-input-start/delta/available, tool-output-available, tool-output-error, reasoning-start/delta/finish, finish, abort, error, metadata (usage/cost).
2. Run them through `piEventsToUIMessageChunks()` (stream adapter) AND through `PiSessionStore.load()` → `UIMessage[]`.
3. Assert both paths produce equivalent semantic content (not strict byte-equal — tool call IDs etc. may differ, but text + tool input/output + reasoning preserved).
4. Assert unknown event types don't crash — pass through as `data-status` warnings.

**File:** `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/__tests__/sessionMapping.conformance.ts`.

**Living fixture:** commit the captured stream as `fixtures/pi-events-corpus.jsonl`. When pi releases new versions, re-capture and re-run.

**Logging:** log event-type distribution in test output (how many of each type covered).

**Acceptance:** 100% of observed pi event types map cleanly; unknowns are warnings not crashes.

## Acceptance Criteria

100% of observed pi event types map cleanly. Unknowns logged as warnings, not crashes.

title: M2: Circuit breaker around Vercel SDK
description:
**Goal:** Wrap the Vercel sandbox client with a circuit breaker to fail fast on sustained API outages. File: `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/circuitBreaker.ts` (~30 LOC).

**Behavior:**
- Tracks recent failure rate (sliding window, 60s).
- States: `closed` (normal) → `open` (fail-fast) → `half-open` (trial request).
- Opens after 5 consecutive failures or 50% failure rate over 20 requests.
- Stays open for 30s, then half-open. Single trial success → closed.
- Exponential backoff on retries within a call: 100ms, 400ms, 1600ms, then give up.

**Why:** Vercel API can flake. Circuit breaker keeps the agent responsive (fast-fail with a clear error) rather than hanging for minutes.

**Tests:** inject failures, assert open after N; open state fails fast; half-open recovery.

## Acceptance Criteria

Opens after 5 consecutive failures. Open state fails fast. Half-open trial success → closed. Exponential backoff within call: 100/400/1600ms then give up.
acceptance_criteria: Opens after 5 consecutive failures. Open state fails fast. Half-open trial success → closed. Exponential backoff within call: 100/400/1600ms then give up.

title: M1: createBwrapSandbox adapter
description:
**Goal:** Linux process-isolation sandbox. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/createBwrapSandbox.ts`.

**Use case:** Single-tenant deployments (boring-macro on Fly). Linux-only.

**Shape:** ~120 LOC.
```ts
export function createBwrapSandbox(): Sandbox {
  let workspace: Workspace
  return {
    id: 'bwrap', placement: 'server', capabilities: ['exec'],
    async init(ctx) { workspace = ctx.workspace; await assertBwrapAvailable() },
    async exec(cmd, opts) {
      const args = [...buildBwrapArgs(workspace.root), 'bash', '-c', cmd]
      return await spawnBoundedBwrap('bwrap', args, { timeoutMs, maxOutputBytes, env, stdin })
    },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- On init: verify `bwrap` binary is on PATH; throw with helpful error if missing.
- Timeout kills via SIGTERM→SIGKILL with grace period.
- Output cap applied post-hoc on stdout/stderr streams.
- Share workspace root via bind (agent writes land in host fs — Workspace + Sandbox see same files).

**Tests:**
- Bwrap happy-path: `echo hello` returns `hello\n` in stdout, exitCode 0.
- File visible to Sandbox is the same file NodeWorkspace writes: write via workspace.writeFile, read via `exec('cat ${path}')`.
- Timeout enforced: `sleep 60` with `timeoutMs: 1000` returns non-zero + duration <~1100ms.
- Output cap: `yes | head -c 10000000` with `maxOutputBytes: 1024` returns `truncated: true`.

**Reference test pattern:** old `packages/workspace/src/server/__tests__/execJob.test.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

On init, verifies bwrap binary on PATH. Bwrap happy path works. Files visible to Sandbox match NodeWorkspace writes. Timeout enforced. maxOutputBytes cap yields truncated: true. Passes shared Sandbox conformance.
acceptance_criteria: On init, verifies bwrap binary on PATH. Bwrap happy path works. Files visible to Sandbox match NodeWorkspace writes. Timeout enforced. maxOutputBytes cap yields truncated: true. Passes shared Sandbox conformance.

title: M3a — Core UX contracts: sessions + resume + bridge
description:
**~4 days.** Load-bearing contracts that must be reliable before polish. If these aren't solid, no amount of chat UX polish helps.

**Deliverables:**
- `SessionStore` interface + `PiSessionStore` over pi's JSONL (list/create/load/delete).
- HTTP: `GET/POST /api/v1/agent/sessions`, `GET/DELETE /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id`.
- Stream resumption: ring buffer per `(sessionId, turnId)` + `GET /api/v1/agent/chat/:sessionId/:turnId?cursor=<n>` replay route. `useChat` with `experimental_resume: true`. ~60 LOC.
- Abort propagation (`useChat.stop()` → pi `abortSignal` → sandbox exec cancellation).
- SSE `file_changed` events when agent writes → frontend invalidates file-tree/file queries.
- UiBridge interface + in-memory impl + SSE fan-out (`/api/v1/ui/state` GET/PUT, `/api/v1/ui/commands` POST, `/api/v1/ui/commands/next` SSE-or-poll). Short-poll fallback via `?poll=true`.
- `get_ui_state` + `exec_ui` tools wired into standardCatalog when uiBridge provided.
- `copyTemplate()` sync helper + idempotency marker `.boring-agent/provisioned`.

title: Phase 3a: Bridge SSE Client
description:
## Goal
SSE subscriber that receives agent commands and dispatches them through the bridge. PUT helper that pushes workspace state to agent server.

## What to implement

### bridge/client.ts — SSE + PUT
#### SSE subscriber (GET /api/v1/ui/commands/next)
- EventSource connection to bridgeEndpoint (configurable via WorkspaceProvider)
- Receives events:
  - event: init — full state snapshot on connect (for reconciliation)
  - event: command — {v:1, kind:'openFile', params:{...}} → dispatch through bridge
  - event: error — {v:1, code, message} → log + show toast
  - event: heartbeat — keep-alive (server sends every 30s), no action
- Protocol version check: all events carry v:1. On mismatch, show warning banner
- Auto-reconnect: EventSource built-in (~3s retry)
- On reconnect: server sends event: init, workspace reconciles with Zustand store (workspace wins)

SSE event format (from plan Section D):
```
event: command
data: {"v":1,"kind":"openFile","params":{"path":"/src/main.ts","mode":"edit"}}

event: command
data: {"v":1,"kind":"showNotification","params":{"msg":"File saved","level":"info"}}

event: error
data: {"v":1,"code":"invalid_command","message":"Unknown panel ID"}

event: heartbeat
data: {}
```

On connect: event: init with full state snapshot for reconciliation.

#### PUT state publisher (PUT /api/v1/ui/state)
- Pushes workspace state after every meaningful state change
- Debounced 100ms (coalesces rapid UI changes)
- Body format (UIStatePut):

```typescript
interface UIStatePut {
  v: 1                                    // protocol version
  causedBy: 'user' | 'agent' | 'restore' // prevents agent echo loops
  openPanels: PanelState[]
  activePanel: string | null
  activeFile: string | null
  visibleFiles: string[]                  // file paths shown in tree
  dirtyFiles: string[]                    // files with unsaved changes
}
```

- 204 No Content response. No validation beyond JSON parsing — workspace is authority.
- causedBy allows agents to distinguish user actions from their own command results.

#### Data flow (command-based, workspace-authoritative)
```
Agent POST {kind:'openFile', params:{path:'/foo.ts'}}
  → agent server validates (Zod per-kind schema)
  → agent server queues command
  → SSE delivers: event: command  data: {v:1, kind:'openFile', params:{...}}
  → workspace executes locally (Zustand store update)
  → workspace PUT /api/v1/ui/state with {v:1, causedBy:'agent', ...}
  → agent reads state via get_ui_state tool

User clicks file in tree:
  → workspace executes locally (Zustand store update)
  → workspace PUT /api/v1/ui/state with {v:1, causedBy:'user', ...}
  → agent reads state via get_ui_state tool
```

Authority: Workspace Zustand store is the source of truth. Agent server is a relay for commands and a cache for state. Server NEVER modifies UI state.

#### Short-poll fallback
- GET /api/v1/ui/commands/next?poll=true — batch of pending commands as JSON
- GET /api/v1/ui/state/latest — one-shot full state snapshot (for reconnection or polling)
- PUT /api/v1/ui/state — workspace pushes state (same endpoint as SSE mode)
- Same JSON format, standard request/response
- Activated when SSE is unavailable (configurable or auto-detected)

#### postMessage transport (for browser-side agents)
- For browser-side agents running in iframe/worker, bridge uses postMessage instead of HTTP
- The bridge abstraction hides the transport — panes and consumers use the same WorkspaceBridge interface regardless of whether the agent is remote (SSE+POST) or local (postMessage)
- This is specified in the plan transport section: "For browser-side agents (iframe/worker), postMessage. The bridge abstraction hides the transport."

### Auth
- Authorization: Bearer <token> header on both SSE and PUT
- Or session cookie
- 401/403 → SSE closes, show reconnection banner
- No special ticket/upgrade mechanism needed (SSE is standard HTTP, not WebSocket)

### Consistency guarantees (from plan Section D)
- **At-most-once command delivery**: Commands are fire-and-forget during disconnect. Agent re-evaluates state and re-issues if needed.
- **Server restart**: All bridge state is in-memory. Restart = clean slate. On reconnect, workspace re-PUTs its current state to the server.
- **Idempotency**: openPanel with duplicate ID activates existing panel (default prefer_existing: true). Makes accidental double-sends harmless.

### Reconnection handling
- SSE EventSource auto-reconnects with browser-default retry interval (~3s)
- On reconnect: server sends event: init with cached state snapshot
- Workspace compares with Zustand store and resolves conflicts (workspace wins — it's authority)
- On reconnect: workspace re-PUTs current state to server

## Endpoints summary (from plan Section D)
```
GET  /api/v1/ui/commands/next  — SSE stream (agent → workspace commands)
POST /api/v1/ui/commands        — agent sends commands
PUT  /api/v1/ui/state           — workspace publishes UI state (workspace → agent)
GET  /api/v1/ui/state/latest    — one-shot full state snapshot (for reconnection or polling)
```

## Considerations
- SSE is standard HTTP — works through any proxy/CDN
- No WebSocket (decision: SSE + POST is simpler, auto-reconnects)
- ~20 LOC server (agent package hosts endpoints, not workspace)
- Bridge client is initialized by WorkspaceProvider when bridgeEndpoint is set
- null bridgeEndpoint = standalone mode (no SSE, no PUT)
- v1 REST bridge endpoints (GET /ui/state, POST /ui/commands) are NOT carried forward — v2 is a hard cut

## v1 references
- uiStateImpl.ts (201 lines) + frontendState.js (2.6KB) — current UI state bridge
- uiStateRoutes.ts (289 lines) — current HTTP endpoints
- Study the state shape: { openPanels, activeFile, commands }
- REPLACE with command-based bridge (tenth pass): SSE event: command stream + PUT /api/v1/ui/state

## Unit tests (bridge/client.test.ts)

### SSE connection
- Test: EventSource connects to bridgeEndpoint
- Test: event: init dispatched as state reconciliation
- Test: event: command dispatched through bridge (openFile, openPanel, etc.)
- Test: event: error logged and toast shown
- Test: event: heartbeat handled (no-op, keeps connection alive)
- Test: protocol version mismatch (v != 1) → warning banner shown

### PUT state publisher
- Test: state change → PUT fires with correct UIStatePut body
- Test: rapid state changes → debounced to single PUT (100ms)
- Test: causedBy field set correctly ('user' for user actions, 'agent' for bridge commands, 'restore' for hydration)
- Test: 204 response handled (no error)

### Short-poll fallback
- Test: poll mode sends GET /api/v1/ui/commands/next?poll=true
- Test: poll returns batch of commands, all dispatched
- Test: poll mode state push via PUT works the same

### Reconnection
- Test: SSE disconnect → auto-reconnect fires
- Test: on reconnect → workspace re-PUTs current state
- Test: on reconnect → init event reconciles with Zustand (workspace wins)
- Test: idempotent openPanel → activates existing panel (no duplicate)

### Auth
- Test: Authorization header set on SSE and PUT
- Test: 401 → SSE closes, reconnection banner shown
- Test: 403 → same behavior as 401

### postMessage transport
- Test: browser-side agent via iframe → postMessage used instead of HTTP
- Test: same WorkspaceBridge interface exposed regardless of transport

## E2E test (bridge protocol end-to-end)
From plan Layer 3 (Playwright E2E):
- Playwright opens workspace UI + a separate HTTP client simulates the agent
- Agent POSTs openFile command → SSE event: command delivers to workspace → workspace applies locally (Zustand) → file panel appears in the UI
- Tests the real SSE/polling path end-to-end (~50 LOC)

## Success criteria
- SSE connection establishes and receives commands
- Commands dispatched through bridge correctly (openFile, openPanel, etc.)
- State pushed via PUT after UI changes (debounced 100ms)
- Reconnection works (re-PUTs state, reconciles with init event)
- Short-poll fallback works when SSE unavailable
- Protocol version mismatch shows warning banner
- Auth errors show reconnection banner

title: M1: File routes (GET/POST/DELETE /api/v1/files, /api/v1/stat, /files/move, /dirs)
description:

` → `workspace.stat(p)` **Tests:** end-to-end per route against a temp NodeWorkspace; traversal rejected; 404 on missing files. **Reference:** Old `packages/workspace/src/server/http/fileRoutes.ts` (~200 LOC) — port structure, adapt fs calls to workspace interface. ## Acceptance Criteria GET/POST/DELETE /api/v1/files roundtrip against temp NodeWorkspace. title: Research: Vercel pricing model — snapshot + VM cost per workspace
description:
**Question:** What does a workspace cost per user per day on Vercel Sandbox? Informs retention + idle policies.

**Reference (from spec):**
- VM time: billed per compute-second. Idle sandboxes hibernate (cheap).
- Snapshot storage: billed per GB-hour.
- API calls: likely free but check.

**Deliverable:** a one-page cost model `docs/VERCEL_COSTS.md` with:
- $/day for a typical user (active chat + idle).
- Snapshot retention trade-off: keep-last-2 is our current default; is that right?
- When to force sandbox expiry (e.g., 24h idle → destroy, force user to re-provision).

**Acceptance:** cost model drafted; retention + idle policies confirmed or revised.

## Acceptance Criteria

Cost model drafted in docs/VERCEL_COSTS.md. Retention + idle policies confirmed or revised.

title: M1+M2-Risk: Pi abort propagation to child processes
description:
**Risk class:** Accepted risk — but we need to know if it's broken. Spec: "Pi's `abortSignal` may not reach child bash processes inside bwrap and Vercel Sandbox."

**Test:** dedicated suite that verifies abort cleanly halts in each mode.

**Per-mode scenarios:**
- **direct:** exec `sleep 30` → abort after 500ms → assert promise rejects with AbortError within 200ms + process exit code indicates SIGTERM.
- **local (bwrap):** same as above; additionally `ps aux | grep bwrap` shows no orphaned processes.
- **vercel-sandbox:** `sleep 30` remotely → abort → assert `sandbox.runCommand` promise rejects; Vercel dashboard shows no runaway tasks.

**If any scenario fails:** document the failure mode in a follow-up risk bead + propose a workaround (timeout-based kill inside the sandbox adapter).

**File:** `src/__tests__/abort-propagation.test.ts`.

**Logging:** `[abort]` prefix; log signal sent, wait time, final status, orphan check output.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 3 modes (direct/bwrap/vercel): abort halts within 200ms. No orphaned processes.

title: Phase 2: Shared Editor Lifecycle Hook
description:
## Goal
useEditorLifecycle() hook (~100 LOC) shared by CodeMirror and Tiptap editors. Eliminates ~80 LOC duplication.

## What to implement

### useEditorLifecycle (src/hooks/useEditorLifecycle.ts)
- Shared between CodeMirrorPane and TiptapPane
- Editor provides adapter: { isDirty: () => boolean, save: () => Promise<void>, getContent: () => string }
- Hook handles:
  1. Dirty state tracking → bridge.markDirty(path) / bridge.markClean(path)
  2. Auto-save debounce (1000ms after last change)
  3. External file change detection (auto-sync when not dirty, suppress stale reads 3s after save)
  4. Tab dirty indicator wiring
  5. flushSave() method (tenth pass): cancels debounce, saves immediately
    - onClose calls flushSave() first
    - If dirty after flush (save failed), prompt user
    - Single save path — onClose never saves independently

## Considerations
- This is the SINGLE save path for both editors
- flushSave() is critical for onClose integration (panel lifecycle)
- External change detection: compare server mtime with last-known mtime

## Success criteria
- Both CodeMirrorPane and MarkdownEditorPane use this hook
- Dirty state correctly tracked and reported to bridge
- Auto-save fires after 1000ms debounce
- flushSave() cancels debounce and saves immediately
- External file changes detected and auto-synced when clean
- Stale read suppression works (3s window after save)

title: M1: CLI entrypoint bin/boring-agent (+ flags + key prompt + gitignore + SSH-aware open)
description:
**Goal:** `npx @boring/agent` experience. File: `bin/boring-agent.ts` (~200 LOC).

**Startup sequence:**
1. Parse flags: `--port <n>`, `--workspace <path>`, `--mode <direct|local|vercel-sandbox>`, `--no-open`, `--logout`, `--reset-key`, `--no-gitignore`, `--verbose`.
2. `--logout` / `--reset-key` → wipe `~/.config/boring-agent/env`, exit.
3. Look up API key: CLI flag > env > `~/.config/boring-agent/env` > interactive prompt → persist to env file.
4. Auto-gitignore: if workspace is a git repo AND `.boring-agent/` not in `.gitignore` → append (skippable via `--no-gitignore`).
5. Port picker: try `--port` (default 3000), auto-increment if occupied (max 10 tries; then error).
6. Load config via `loadConfig()` (see `gov.config-schema`).
7. Boot Fastify + mount static `dist/frontend/`.
8. SSH/headless detection: `$SSH_CONNECTION` set OR `$DISPLAY` missing OR `$WAYLAND_DISPLAY` missing → print URL only. Else `open <url>` (macOS) / `xdg-open <url>` (Linux).
9. Print banner: `@boring/agent v${version} — workspace: <root> — mode: <m>`.
10. Graceful shutdown: SIGINT/SIGTERM → abort any in-flight streams, close server, dispose sandbox handles, flush logs.

**API key persistence (`~/.config/boring-agent/env` format):**
```
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
# Added by boring-agent CLI on <date>
```
- File mode: `0600` (user-only readable).
- Directory: `~/.config/boring-agent/` auto-created with `0700`.
- Loading: `fs.readFile()` + parse KEY=VALUE lines; supports `#` comments.

**CLI flags table:**
| Flag | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `--port <n>` | 3000 | auto-increment on conflict |
| `--workspace <path>` | cwd | workspace root |
| `--mode <m>` | auto-detect | direct/local/vercel-sandbox |
| `--no-open` | false | skip browser open |
| `--logout` / `--reset-key` | — | wipe stored API key, exit |
| `--no-gitignore` | false | skip auto-gitignore |
| `--verbose` / `-v` | false | debug logging |
| `--help` / `-h` | — | print usage |
| `--version` | — | print version |

**Windows:** unsupported natively (spec decision). Use WSL2 → Linux path works.

**Tests:**
- `--logout` wipes env file; second boot prompts again.
- Port picker skips occupied ports; errors after 10 tries.
- SSH detection: set `SSH_CONNECTION=x`, assert no browser-open attempt.
- Signal handlers: send SIGINT, assert clean shutdown within 1s.
- API key file: mode 0600, correct parsing.
- Auto-gitignore: in git repo w/o `.boring-agent/` → added. In non-repo → skip silently.

**Logging:** `[cli]` prefix. Log port selection, mode resolution, workspace root, API key source (flag/env/file/prompt — never the key itself).

## Acceptance Criteria

`npx @boring/agent` in a random directory opens a browser (or prints URL via SSH detection), answers 'list files in /tmp' with bash tool call + streamed reply.
acceptance_criteria: `npx @boring/agent` in a random directory opens a browser (or prints URL via SSH detection), answers 'list files in /tmp' with bash tool call + streamed reply.

title: Phase 2: File Tree Pane
description:
## Goal
Virtualized file tree using react-arborist. Standalone FileTree component (props-only) + FileTreePane wrapper (dockview + context).

## What to implement

### FileTree (src/components/FileTree.tsx) — STANDALONE, Tier 3
- Props-only, zero context dependency (tenth pass contract)
- Props: files: FileEntry[], selectedPath?, expandedDirs?, searchQuery?, onSelect?, onExpand?, onCollapse?, onContextMenu?, onDragDrop?
- Uses react-arborist (~15KB gz) for virtualized rendering
- O(visible) rendering, handles 10K+ files
- Keyboard navigation (arrow keys expand/collapse/navigate) — provided by react-arborist
- ARIA tree semantics (role=tree/treeitem/aria-expanded) — provided by react-arborist
- Drag-and-drop reordering (file/folder onto directory)
- Collapsible directories, file icons (lucide-react)
- No git status badges (agent responsibility)
- Flat tree, single root, no section system

### FileTreePane (src/panes/FileTreePane.tsx) — dockview wrapper
- Reads file list from DataProvider (useFileList hook)
- Polling: refetchInterval 3000ms for file list, paused during edits
- Search/filter input at top (shadcn Input) with debounced search (200ms)
  - Client-side filter for <5K files
  - Falls back to server-side /api/v1/files/search for larger workspaces
- Context menu (shadcn DropdownMenu):
  - New file (POST /api/v1/files)
  - New folder (POST /api/v1/dirs)
  - Rename (POST /api/v1/files/move)
  - Delete (DELETE /api/v1/files) — with AlertDialog confirmation
  - Copy path (client-only clipboard)
  - Open to side (bridge command)
- Drag-and-drop file moving via POST /api/v1/files/move
  - Drop validation (can't drop into self/children)
  - Visual drag-over feedback
- Wires onSelect to bridge.openFile()
- Lazy-loaded via dynamic import

## v1 references
- FileTree.jsx (1129 lines) + FileTreePanel.jsx (344 lines)
- Rewrite: replace custom CSS with shadcn, use react-arborist instead of custom tree

## Success criteria
- FileTree renders 10K+ files without lag (virtualized)
- Keyboard navigation works (arrows, enter to open)
- Context menu actions: create, rename, delete, copy path
- Drag-and-drop moves files correctly
- Search filters client-side for small trees, server-side for large
- Standalone FileTree works without WorkspaceProvider (props-only)
- FileTreePane works inside dockview with DataProvider context

title: Infra: CI pipeline (lint + typecheck + unit + e2e + invariants)
description:

title: M2: Periodic snapshot cron (10 min / on-idle)
description:
**Goal:** Background task per active sandbox that snapshots every ~10 min (or on idle) for crash-recovery safety. File: `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/periodicSnapshot.ts`.

**Behavior:**
- Per workspaceId/sandbox pair, schedule `setInterval(() => sandbox.snapshot(), 10 * 60 * 1000)`.
- Skip snapshot if no activity in last 10 min (detect via "dirty" marker set on writes).
- On snapshot: update `SandboxHandleStore` with new snapshotId.
- Stopped on sandbox dispose or backend shutdown.

**Accepted risk:** no on-dirty-turn-end snapshot — cost amplification risk in chatty sessions. Periodic only.

**Tests:** cron triggers snapshot; dirty skip works; graceful shutdown cancels pending timers.

## Acceptance Criteria

Cron triggers snapshot at interval. Dirty skip works for idle sessions. Graceful shutdown cancels pending timers.
acceptance_criteria: Cron triggers snapshot at interval. Dirty skip works for idle sessions. Graceful shutdown cancels pending timers.

title: Gov: Audit — every user-facing piece follows default+primitives+hook pattern
description:

**Audit targets:**
| User piece | Default | Primitives | Hook |
|---|---|---|---|
| Chat | `<ChatPanel />` | `<Message />`, `<Composer />`, `<Tool />`, `<Terminal />`, `<CodeBlock />`, `<Reasoning />` | `useAgentChat()` |
| Sessions | `<SessionToolbar />` | (reuses shadcn `Dropdown`) | `useSessions()` |
| Slash commands | built-ins in Composer | `<SlashCommandList />` (if we add) | `useSlashCommands()` / `registerCommand()` |
| Model/thinking | `<ModelPicker />`, `<ThinkingToggle />` | (reuses shadcn `Select`) | model + thinking are inputs to `useAgentChat` |
| UI bridge (client) | — (workspace owns display) | — | `useUiBridge()` (if needed by workspace) |

**Enforcement:** pre-release audit checklist; fail the milestone gate if any piece is missing one of the three.

**Acceptance:** checklist in `docs/COMPONENT_PATTERNS.md`; audit run at M5 gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

Checklist in `docs/COMPONENT_PATTERNS.md`. Audit run at M5 gate. Every user-facing piece has default + primitives + hook.

title: M3b: Copy ai-elements into src/front/primitives/
description:
**Goal:** Adopt Vercel's ai-elements shadcn-style — copy sources into our tree so we own them. Files: `src/front/primitives/{Message,Composer,Tool,Terminal,CodeBlock,Reasoning}.tsx`.

**Why copy (vs. import as package):** shadcn philosophy — shipped code is ours to modify. ai-elements evolves; we freeze our version. Matches our existing shadcn/ui adoption pattern.

**Files to copy + adapt:**
- `Message` (role-based layout)
- `PromptInput` → rename to `Composer`
- `Tool` (generic tool call renderer with expand/collapse)
- `Terminal` (for bash stdout/stderr)
- `CodeBlock` (syntax highlighting for read)
- `Reasoning` (collapsible thinking blocks)

**Each file gets a provenance comment at the top:**
```ts
/**
 * Adapted from @ai-sdk/react's ai-elements (vercel-labs/ai).
 * Source: https://github.com/vercel-labs/ai/tree/main/packages/ai-elements
 * Copied: <date>. We own this file; upstream updates require re-port.
 */
```

**Adaptations:**
- Classnames → CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-message-bg`, etc.) instead of Tailwind utilities only, OR dual-system Tailwind v4 + vars.
- Replace `lucide-react` with already-adopted icon set.

title: Phase 1: Zustand Store + Persistence Engine
description:
## Goal
Single Zustand store (store/index.ts) with partitioned persist middleware. Two localStorage keys: layout (per-workspace) and preferences (global). Bridge state is ephemeral (not persisted).

## Background (tenth pass decision)
One useWorkspaceStore(). Persist middleware uses partialize to route: layout→localStorage, preferences→localStorage, bridge state→ephemeral. store/index.ts is the single file. persistence/ and bridge/ are thin modules reading/writing this store.

## What to implement

### store/index.ts — the single Zustand store
- NOT exported from public API (enforced by API design)
- State shape:
  - hydrationComplete: boolean
  - layout: SerializedDockviewLayout | null
  - sidebar: { collapsed: boolean, width: number }
  - panelSizes: Record<string, number>
  - preferences: { theme: 'light' | 'dark' }
  - Bridge state (ephemeral, NOT persisted):
    - panels: PanelState[]
    - activePanel: string | null
    - activeFile: string | null
    - visibleFiles: string[]
    - dirtyFiles: Map<string, { panelId: string, savedAt: number | null }>
    - notifications: Notification[]
- Actions: openFile, openPanel, closePanel, showNotification, navigateToLine, markDirty, markClean, setHydrationComplete, etc.

### store/selectors.ts — atomic hooks (the ONLY public store API)
- useActiveFile() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.activeFile)
- useActivePanel() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.activePanel)
- useSidebarState() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.sidebar)
- useOpenPanels() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.panels)
- useDirtyFiles() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.dirtyFiles)
- These are the ONLY exported hooks. useWorkspaceStore is internal.

### Persistence middleware configuration
- Two partitions via partialize:
  - boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId} — layout, sidebar, panelSizes (reset on schema change)
  - boring-ui-v2:preferences — theme (persists forever, never reset)
- workspaceId falls back to omitted (key = boring-ui-v2:layout) if not provided
- Version field: '2.0' (string). Mismatch → call onLayoutVersionMismatch() callback (default: reset)

### Persistence hardening
- Zod schema validation on restore: reject negative dimensions, path separators in panel IDs, strings >1KB
- QuotaExceededError handling: try/catch on localStorage.setItem(). On quota error, clear layout key and retry once. If still failing, disable persistence for session + toast.
- Cross-tab: listen to window.addEventListener('storage') for layout key changes. Do NOT auto-apply (last-writer-wins, documented). Just log.
- Size budget: target <50KB serialized. Log warning if >100KB.

### persistence/useLayoutPersistence.ts
- Hook that wires dockview onDidLayoutChange → store
- Debounced 300ms (onDidLayoutChange fires per-pixel during sash drags)
- Flush on beforeunload
- Auto-restore on mount via Zustand onRehydrateStorage

### Hydration gate (Risk 7 — CRITICAL)
- onRehydrateStorage fires → set hydrationComplete = true
- DockviewShell gates onReady behind this flag (see dockview bead)
- If not hydrated yet → queue onReady, execute after hydration
- Show LoadingSkeleton until hydrated

Hydration gate pseudo-code (from plan Risk 7 mitigation):

```typescript
// store/index.ts — single store, partitioned persist (tenth pass)
export const useWorkspaceStore = create<WorkspaceStoreState>()(
  persist(
    (set, get) => ({
      hydrationComplete: false,
      layout: null,
      // ... other state
    }),
    {
      name: 'boring-ui-v2:layout',
      onRehydrateStorage: () => (state) => {
        // Fires AFTER hydration completes
        state?.setHydrationComplete(true)
      },
    }
  )
)
```

DockviewShell integration (consuming the hydration gate):

```typescript
// layouts/DockviewShell.tsx
function DockviewShell(props: DockviewShellProps) {
  const hydrationComplete = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.hydrationComplete)
  const pendingOnReady = useRef<DockviewReadyEvent | null>(null)

  const handleReady = useCallback((event: DockviewReadyEvent) => {
    if (!hydrationComplete) {
      pendingOnReady.current = event
      return
    }
    initializeDockview(event)
  }, [hydrationComplete])

  // Execute queued onReady when hydration completes
  useEffect(() => {
    if (hydrationComplete && pendingOnReady.current) {
      initializeDockview(pendingOnReady.current)
      pendingOnReady.current = null
    }
  }, [hydrationComplete])

  if (!hydrationComplete) return <LoadingSkeleton />

  return <DockviewReact onReady={handleReady} ... />
}
```

### Zod schema validation specifics
- Layout partition restore validation:
  - All dimension numbers (width, height, minWidth, etc.) must be >= 0
  - Panel IDs must be alphanumeric + dash, max 64 chars (no path separators, no null bytes)
  - String fields max 1KB (prevents oversized data injection)
  - layout field must be valid DockviewSerializedLayout shape (groups array, panels map)
  - sidebar.width must be positive integer
  - theme must be 'light' | 'dark' (reject unknown strings)
- On validation failure: log error, return null (triggers default state), toast "Layout restored with defaults"

## localStorage key inventory (namespace collision avoidance)
v1 scatters state across 12+ keys. v2 consolidates to two keys:

| v1 key pattern | v2 status |
|----------------|-----------|
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:layout | → boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId} |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:tabs | Dropped (derived from layout) |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:lastKnownGoodLayout | Dropped |
| boring-ui-theme / kurt-web-theme | → boring-ui-v2:preferences |
| boring-ui:chat-sessions:v1 | Agent package responsibility |
| boring-ui:terminal-* | Dropped (terminal → agent) |

## v1 references
- LayoutManager.js (618 lines) — save/restore, toJSON serialization, validation
- DROP: version migration system, lastKnownGoodLayout backup, multi-key storage
- KEEP: toJSON/fromJSON round-trip, validation pattern

## Risk mitigations embedded
- **Risk 4** (Persistence resets during development): Schema changes are rare after Phase 1 stabilizes. Simple version check (version !== current → reset) is already in place. No migration system needed.
- **Risk 7** (Zustand hydration race — CRITICAL): See hydration gate pseudo-code above. Zustand persist middleware onRehydrateStorage fires AFTER hydration. DockviewShell gates onReady behind hydrationComplete flag. Cleaner than v1's ref-based approach.
- **Risk 12** (Persisted layout shadows new LayoutConfig): LayoutConfig includes version field. Persisted layout stores the config version. On restore, versions differ → call onLayoutVersionMismatch() callback (defaults to reset). Apps can provide custom migration.

## Unit tests (store/persistence.test.ts)

### Store initialization
- Test: store initializes with correct default shape (all fields present, hydrationComplete=false)
- Test: bridge state fields start as empty (panels=[], activeFile=null, dirtyFiles empty)

### Persistence middleware
- Test: layout partition writes to localStorage under correct key (boring-ui-v2:layout)
- Test: preferences partition writes to localStorage under correct key (boring-ui-v2:preferences)
- Test: workspaceId scoping: different workspaceId → different layout key
- Test: bridge state (panels, activeFile, activePanel) is NOT written to localStorage
- Test: theme persists independently of layout (changing layout key doesn't affect theme)

### Zod validation on restore
- Test: valid layout JSON → restores correctly
- Test: negative dimensions → rejected, defaults applied
- Test: panel IDs with path separators (../../) → rejected
- Test: strings >1KB → rejected
- Test: missing required fields → rejected, defaults applied
- Test: corrupted JSON (not valid JSON) → rejected, defaults applied

### QuotaExceededError handling
- Test: mock localStorage.setItem throwing QuotaExceededError → clears key, retries
- Test: second QuotaExceededError → disables persistence for session
- Test: toast notification fires on persistent quota error

### Hydration gate
- Test: hydrationComplete starts false
- Test: onRehydrateStorage → hydrationComplete becomes true
- Test: subscribers notified when hydrationComplete changes

### Cross-tab storage events
- Test: storage event on layout key from other tab → logged (not applied)
- Test: storage event on unrelated key → ignored

### Size budget
- Test: serialized state >100KB → warning logged
- Test: serialized state <50KB → no warning

### Atomic selector hooks
- Test: useActiveFile returns correct value
- Test: useActivePanel returns correct value
- Test: useSidebarState returns correct value
- Test: useOpenPanels returns correct value
- Test: useDirtyFiles returns correct value
- Test: selectors only re-render when their specific slice changes

## Success criteria
- Zustand store initializes with correct shape
- Persist middleware writes to correct localStorage keys
- workspaceId scoping works (different keys per workspace)
- Hydration gate blocks DockviewShell until store is ready
- Zod validation rejects corrupted state gracefully (fallback to defaults)
- QuotaExceededError handled (disable persistence + toast, not crash)
- Atomic selector hooks return correct slices
- useWorkspaceStore is NOT importable from package public API

title: M2: vercel-sandbox RuntimeModeAdapter
description:
**Goal:** Self-contained `vercel-sandbox` mode module. File: `src/server/runtime/modes/vercel-sandbox.ts`.

**Shape:**
```ts
export const vercelSandboxMode: RuntimeModeAdapter = {
  id: 'vercel-sandbox',
  async create(ctx) {
    const sandbox = await resolveSandboxHandle(ctx.workspaceId, store, vercelClient)
    const workspace = createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox)
    const exec = createVercelSandboxExec(sandbox)
    await exec.init({ workspace, sessionId: ctx.sessionId })
    return { workspace, sandbox: exec, fileSearch: createServerFileSearch(workspace, exec), uiBridge: ctx.uiBridge }
  },
}
```

**Config (required for this mode):**
- `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN` — auth token (short-lived; see `risk.oidc-refresh`)
- `VERCEL_TEAM_ID` — billing scope
- `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox` — explicit opt-in

**Invariants:**
- Pairing via construction (same handle passed to workspace + exec).
- `await exec.init({workspace, sessionId})` — init hook for any one-time bootstrap (e.g., cd to /vercel/sandbox, set git config).

**Cold start + pricing reference (from spec §Vercel Sandbox):**
- **Cold start:** 2–10s for `Sandbox.create({source:git})` without a snapshot; sub-second with snapshot. See `research.vercel-cold-start-measure` for real numbers.
- **Hibernation:** VMs hibernate after 5 min idle; resume is ~1–2s.
- **Pricing model:** billed per compute-second (active) + snapshot storage (GB-hour). Idle hibernated VMs are essentially free.
- **API calls:** metered but cheap.
- **Container images:** `node24`, `node22`, `python3.13` available; changeable via `[sandbox.vercel].runtime`.

**Why Vercel vs Cloudflare Sandbox (spec §Future modes):** Vercel ships Firecracker + fs APIs. Cloudflare Sandbox is a potential parallel adapter; not in v1.

**Tests:**
- Mode creates a working bundle against a mocked VercelSandbox.
- Env var check failures produce helpful error messages (`VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN is required for vercel-sandbox mode`).
- Pairing: write via workspace, read via exec — same content.

**Logging:** `[vercel-sandbox:mode]` prefix; log each handle create/resume with sandboxId + snapshotId.

## Acceptance Criteria

Mode creates working bundle against mocked VercelSandbox. Missing env vars produce helpful errors. Pairing verified: write via workspace → read via exec same content.
acceptance_criteria: Mode creates working bundle against mocked VercelSandbox. Missing env vars produce helpful errors. Pairing verified: write via workspace → read via exec same content.

title: Phase 2: Markdown Editor Pane (Tiptap)
description:
## Goal
Port v1's tiptap editor with 10 extensions (reduced from 16). Standalone MarkdownEditor + dockview pane wrapper.

## What to implement

### MarkdownEditor (src/components/MarkdownEditor.tsx) — STANDALONE, Tier 3

title: Research: Vercel Sandbox cold-start + snapshot UX measurement
description:
**Question:** How long does first workspace creation take with snapshot vs without? What's the right "restoring workspace…" UX?

**Investigation:**
1. Measure cold start: `Sandbox.create({source: git-url})` with no snapshot → time to first command-execable.
2. Measure warm start: `Sandbox.create({source: snapshotId})` → time to first command-execable.
3. Measure snapshot creation time (at end of turn).

**Data drives:**
- `risk.vercel-cold-start-ux` — if cold start > 3s, the async-polling UX is definitely needed.
- Snapshot retention policy: if snapshots take >5s, we can't afford too many.

**Acceptance:** numbers in `docs/PERFORMANCE.md`; thresholds + mitigation budget set.

## Acceptance Criteria

Numbers in docs/PERFORMANCE.md. Cold vs warm vs snapshot-creation timings confirmed.

title: M3a: UI bridge routes (/api/v1/ui/*) — SSE + poll
description:
**Goal:** HTTP surface for the bridge. File: `src/server/http/routes/ui.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Routes:**
- `GET /api/v1/ui/state` → `bridge.getState()`.
- `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` body `{state, causedBy: 'user'|'agent'|'restore'}` → `bridge.setState(body.state)`. Ignore echoes based on causedBy.
- `GET /api/v1/ui/state/latest` → cached snapshot (same as GET /state; separate route for polling clients).
- `POST /api/v1/ui/commands` body `{kind, params}` → `bridge.postCommand(cmd)` → returns `{seq, status}`.
- `GET /api/v1/ui/commands/next` (SSE) → emits `event: command` for each bridge event. `?poll=true` → returns batch JSON `{commands: [...]}` and clears queue.

**Zod schemas** for command kinds (openFile, openPanel, showNotification, navigateToLine, expandToFile + extensible fallback).

**Tests:** state PUT+GET roundtrip; POST command → SSE subscriber receives; poll fallback returns queue batch + clears.

## Acceptance Criteria

state PUT+GET roundtrip. POST command → SSE subscriber receives. `?poll=true` returns queue batch + clears.

title: M2-Risk: OIDC token refresh for Vercel SDK
description:
**Risk class:** Requires real mitigation (spec §Risks > Risks requiring real mitigation in v1).

**Problem:** Vercel Sandbox SDK authenticates via `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`. OIDC tokens have short lifetimes (minutes to an hour). If the token expires mid-agent-session, every subsequent API call 401s → agent errors → user sees cryptic failures.

**Mitigation:**
- Intercept Vercel SDK 401/403 responses.
- Trigger a token refresh (exchange long-lived credentials or CI credentials for a new OIDC token). Reference: Vercel Sandbox docs on OIDC federation.
- Retry the failed call once with the fresh token.
- If refresh itself fails → surface a clear error to the user ("Vercel auth expired; restart with fresh `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`").

**File:** `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/oidcRefresh.ts` (~40 LOC), injected into circuit breaker middleware.

**Tests:**
- Mock 401 on first call → refresh fires → second call succeeds.
- Mock sustained 401 → refresh fails → user-facing error with stable code (`OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED`).
- Token cached; second call within TTL doesn't re-refresh.

**Logging:** `[oidc]` prefix on refresh events; log token expiry + next-refresh-at timestamp. Redact actual token bytes.

**Acceptance:** sustained 30-min agent session succeeds even when each OIDC token lasts only 5 min.

## Acceptance Criteria

Sustained 30-min session succeeds even when OIDC tokens last only 5 min. Refresh-fail error has stable code OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED.

title: M0: Interface — Workspace + Entry + Stat
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 workspace contract. File: `src/shared/workspace.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export interface Workspace {
  readonly root: string                           // absolute path or opaque URI (browser)
  readFile(relPath: string): Promise<string>
  writeFile(relPath: string, data: string): Promise<void>
  unlink(relPath: string): Promise<void>          // removes a file OR an empty directory
  readdir(relPath: string): Promise<Entry[]>
  stat(relPath: string): Promise<Stat>
  mkdir(relPath: string, opts?: { recursive?: boolean }): Promise<void>
  rename(fromRelPath: string, toRelPath: string): Promise<void>   // handles drag-to-move AND in-place rename
}
export interface Entry { name: string; kind: 'file' | 'dir' }
export interface Stat { size: number; mtimeMs: number; kind: 'file' | 'dir' }
```

**Notes:**
- Platform-agnostic so future OPFS/IndexedDB browser impl satisfies it.
- Every impl MUST reject paths that escape workspace root (via realpath check, not string manipulation). Security-critical.
- `rename` unifies in-place rename and drag-and-drop move — same op, path change only.
- `unlink` removes files AND empty directories (matches `node:fs/promises.rm` single-entry semantics).

**Tests:** Type-level stub. A shared conformance test suite lands in the infra epic (see `infra.conformance-tests`).

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/workspace.ts` exports `Workspace`, `Entry`, `Stat`. Includes all 7 methods: readFile/writeFile/unlink/readdir/stat/mkdir/rename. Type-level stub passes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/workspace.ts` exports `Workspace`, `Entry`, `Stat`. Includes all 7 methods: readFile/writeFile/unlink/readdir/stat/mkdir/rename. Type-level stub passes.

title: M3b: Background session title generation
description:
**Goal:** After the first assistant reply, generate a short title for the session. Fallback: `"New chat {date}"`.

**Flow:** on first turn complete, kick off a background request to LLM (cheap model: haiku) with prompt: `"Summarize this conversation in 5 words or less for a session title: {first user message} / {first assistant reply}"`. Write title back via SessionStore.

**Defer if it complicates M3b:** fallback is already good UX.

**Tests:** first turn → title populates within ~5s; falls back gracefully if LLM call fails.

## Acceptance Criteria

First turn → title populates within ~5s. LLM failure → fallback title. Title flows to SessionStore.

title: M2-Risk: Vercel cold-start UX (async provisioning + polling)
description:
**Risk class:** Requires real mitigation. Spec §Risks warns: first `Sandbox.create({source:'git'})` takes 2–10s; agents/users experience this as a frozen UI.

**Problem in agent v1 (no `/workspaces` route):** workspaces are bound to the server process via env. But first POST /api/v1/agent/chat to a newly-booted backend will lazily resolve the sandbox handle, hitting cold start inline — user's first message appears to hang.

**Mitigation (agent-v1 scope — simplified, since we dropped the state-machine route):**
- On backend boot in `vercel-sandbox` mode: kick off `resolveSandboxHandle(BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID)` asynchronously. Emit explicit status events (`restoring workspace`, `ready`) via a new SSE topic `/api/v1/ready-status`.
- Frontend subscribes to this on mount; shows a "Preparing workspace…" card above ChatPanel until status is `ready`.
- Chat route returns 503 with `{retryAfter: 2}` while status is not ready.

**Why not the full async-provisioning state machine:** agent v1 is single-workspace-per-backend; workspace CRUD belongs to `@boring/cloud`. The simpler pattern above achieves the UX without a full state machine.

**Tests:**
- Mocked cold-start: first chat before ready → 503. After ready event → 200 stream.
- Warm start (snapshot cached): ready event fires <500ms.
- E2E with real Vercel: cold start UX shows the "Preparing" card then disappears.

**Logging:** `[ready-status]` prefix; log each state transition with timestamp.

## Acceptance Criteria

Cold start: chat route returns 503 while sandbox not ready; 'Preparing workspace' card shown. After ready event: 200 stream. Warm start: ready event <500ms.

```ts
export interface FileSearch {

```

**Why separate from Workspace:**
- Server mode impl uses `sandbox.exec('find . -name …')` — needs both Workspace AND Sandbox refs.

**Used by:** `/api/v1/files/search` route + potential future `grep`/`glob` agent tools.

## Acceptance Criteria

description:

**Interface:**

```ts
export interface AgentHarness {
  readonly id: string                             // 'pi-coding-agent' | 'pi-agent-core' | 'tool-loop'
  readonly placement: 'server' | 'browser'

}
```

**Tests:** Type-level stub: `expectTypeOf<AgentHarness>().toMatchTypeOf<{...}>()`.

title: Infra: Invariant grep checks (no Node imports in shared)
description:
**Goal:** Prevent regressions on the invariants stated in spec §Invariants to preserve. Script: `scripts/check-invariants.sh` wired into `pnpm ci`.

**The 4 invariants (verbatim from spec lines 219-227):**
1. **No `node:fs` / `node:child_process` imports in routes, catalog, or tools.** These Node APIs are allowed only inside adapter implementations (`server/workspace/**`, `server/harness/**`, `server/sandbox/**`, `server/runtime/**`). Grep-enforceable.
2. **Routes and tools both receive `Workspace` as a parameter** (not a path, not a root dir). Resolution to a specific adapter is the job of `resolveMode` — centralized.
3. **Per-request, agent tools and HTTP routes share the same adapter instance.** Ensures a read concurrent with a write sees each other's effects without cache-coherence games.
4. **Path validation is the adapter's responsibility.** Consumers pass user-supplied paths; the adapter rejects `../` / absolute / symlink-escape attacks internally.

**Grep-enforced checks (all fail CI on violation):**
- No `import .*'node:'` OR `from 'node:'` OR `require\('node:'` in `src/shared/**`.
- No `Buffer\b` in `src/shared/**` (must use `Uint8Array`).
- No `import .*'node:fs'` or `'node:child_process'` in `src/server/http/routes/**` or `src/server/catalog/**`.
- No `import .*'@boring/agent/server'` in `src/front/**` (frontend-server bleed).
- No raw `console.log` in `src/server/**` (must use structured logger — see `infra.logging-standard`).
- No `process.env.` reads outside of `src/server/config/**` (config centralization).

**Runtime-enforced checks (runtime assertion, not grep):**
- Workspace methods always validate paths (cheap assertion: reject absolute paths at the adapter wrapper).
- RuntimeModeAdapter contract: `workspace.root === sandbox.cwd` (or equivalent invariant proven by construction).

**Tests:** bad-import fixtures under `test-fixtures/invariants-bad/` should make the check fail; good fixtures should pass.

**Logging:** `[invariants]` prefix; on violation: print exact file + line + invariant name + fix hint.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 4 invariants grep-enforced. Zero violations on main. PR check blocks merges with violations. Bad-import fixtures cause failure.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 invariants grep-enforced. Zero violations on main. PR check blocks merges with violations. Bad-import fixtures cause failure.

title: M2: createVercelSandboxWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** `Workspace` adapter backed by Vercel Sandbox's file APIs. File: `src/server/workspace/createVercelSandboxWorkspace.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox: VercelSandbox): Workspace {
  return {
    root: '/vercel/sandbox',
    async readFile(rel) { const p = assertSafe(rel); return sandbox.fs.readFile(p, 'utf-8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... sandbox.writeFiles([{path, content}]) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.rm(p) },
    async readdir(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.readdir(p) → Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.stat(p) → Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... sandbox.fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { ... sandbox.fs.rename(absFrom, absTo) },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- Accepts a `VercelSandbox` handle — NOT a Vercel client — so it shares the handle with `VercelSandboxExec`. Enforces pairing invariant.
- Path validation via same `assertSafe` helper (shared with NodeWorkspace; no relative paths leaking to VM).
- Returns `Uint8Array` at the boundary; `readFile` decodes UTF-8 for agent consumption.

**Tests:** conformance suite (same as NodeWorkspace). Verifies shared-handle with exec sees same files.

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite against a mocked Vercel sandbox. Writes via workspace visible to paired exec on same handle.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite against a mocked Vercel sandbox. Writes via workspace visible to paired exec on same handle.

title: M3a: E2E — session CRUD + resume + bridge reliability
description:

title: Research: Vercel Sandbox latency on tight fs loops
description:
**Question:** How slow is Vercel Sandbox for agent patterns like "list 100 files" or "grep across 50 files"? Each `exec` has per-call overhead.

**Investigation:**
1. Benchmark: 100-file tree listing via `sandbox.exec('find . -type f')`.
2. Benchmark: 50-file grep via `sandbox.exec('grep -r pattern .')`.
3. Compare vs local bwrap same commands.

**If painful (>10s for common operations):**
- Add sandbox-side `grep` tool that batches reads.
- Cache directory structure in-process.
- Introduce a `find_files` tool over the FileSearch abstraction instead of `bash find`.

**Acceptance:** benchmark numbers documented; mitigations beaded if >2x slower than local.

## Acceptance Criteria

Benchmark numbers in docs/PERFORMANCE.md. Mitigations beaded if >2x slower than local.

title: Phase 4: Error Handling + Recovery
description:
## Goal
2-tier error recovery (pane boundary + full reset), network error handling, corrupted state handling.

## What to implement

### Network errors
- HTTP fetch wrapper: auto-retry 3x with exponential backoff for 5xx / network errors
- 401/403: fire onAuthError callback
- Timeout: 10s default, configurable per route, toast on timeout
- Offline detection: navigator.onLine + fetch probe, show banner, disable writes

### Panel crashes (Tier 1)
- React error boundary per pane (src/components/PanelErrorBoundary.tsx)
- Crashed pane shows error + retry button, doesn't kill workspace
- Error logged with panel ID and stack trace
- Bridge notified: bridge.emit('pane:error', { panelId, error })

### Corrupted state (Tier 2)
- Zod validate layout JSON on rehydration
- Invalid layout (missing fields, negative dimensions): reset to defaults + toast
- Corrupted panel references (non-existent component): strip unknown panels, keep valid
- Reset button in UI: user can force-reset layout from workspace menu

### Recovery hierarchy (2-tier)
1. Retry (panel error boundary — per-pane)

title: M1: Chat route (POST /api/v1/agent/chat)
description:
**Goal:** Agent chat endpoint with UIMessage streaming. File: `src/server/http/routes/chat.ts`.

**Request body:**
```ts
{
  sessionId: string,
  message: string,
  model?: 'sonnet' | 'haiku' | 'opus',
  thinkingLevel?: 'off' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high',
}
```

**Flow:**
1. Resolve `RuntimeBundle` via `resolveMode(config.mode)` — cached per session.
2. Resolve `harness` (PiCodingAgentHarness with `standardCatalog(bundle)` tools).
3. Call `harness.sendMessage(body, { workspaceId, abortSignal })` — returns `AsyncIterable<UIMessageChunk>`.
4. Stream via `createUIMessageStream` (AI SDK) → HTTP response.
5. Track `(sessionId, turnId, chunkIdx)` in ring buffer for resume (integration point; buffer infra itself is M3a).

**Headers:** `Content-Type: text/event-stream`; `Cache-Control: no-cache`.

**Abort handling:** link HTTP request aborts → AbortController passed as `abortSignal`. Flow through to pi → sandbox.exec.

**Tests:** roundtrip with mock harness asserts SSE chunks arrive; abort mid-stream halts within 200ms; invalid body → 400 structured error.

## Acceptance Criteria

Roundtrip with mock harness asserts SSE chunks arrive. Abort mid-stream halts within 200ms. Invalid body → 400 structured error.
acceptance_criteria: Roundtrip with mock harness asserts SSE chunks arrive. Abort mid-stream halts within 200ms. Invalid body → 400 structured error.

title: M1: RuntimeModeAdapter + resolveMode factory
description:
**Goal:** Each execution mode (`direct` | `local` | `vercel-sandbox`) is a self-contained module producing a `RuntimeBundle`. File: `src/server/runtime/modes/{direct,local,vercel-sandbox}.ts` + `src/server/runtime/resolveMode.ts`.

**Contract:**
```ts
export interface RuntimeModeAdapter {
  readonly id: 'direct' | 'local' | 'vercel-sandbox'
  create(ctx: ModeContext): Promise<RuntimeBundle>
}
export interface RuntimeBundle {
  workspace: Workspace
  sandbox: Sandbox
  fileSearch: FileSearch
  uiBridge?: UiBridge                  // pass-through from app-level
}
export interface ModeContext {
  workspaceRoot: string
  sessionId: string
  uiBridge?: UiBridge
}
```

**v1 in-scope modes (this task):** `direct` + `local` only. `vercel-sandbox` lands in M2.

**Auto-detection:**
```ts
function autoDetectMode(): 'direct' | 'local' | 'vercel-sandbox' {
  if (process.env.BORING_AGENT_MODE) return process.env.BORING_AGENT_MODE as any
  if (process.platform === 'linux' && hasBwrap()) return 'local'
  return 'direct'                      // cross-platform fallback
}
```

**Pairing invariant:** each mode owns BOTH workspace and sandbox construction → impossible to build a mismatched pair. Shared fs substrate guaranteed by construction.

**Tests:**
- `resolveMode('direct')` returns a bundle where `workspace` is a NodeWorkspace and `sandbox` is a DirectSandbox.
- `resolveMode('local')` returns NodeWorkspace + BwrapSandbox. Gracefully errors on non-Linux.
- Writing via `bundle.workspace.writeFile` is visible via `bundle.sandbox.exec('cat …')` — proves shared substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria

resolveMode('direct') and resolveMode('local') produce RuntimeBundles where workspace.writeFile + sandbox.exec('cat') see same content. Auto-detection: Linux+bwrap→local, else→direct.
acceptance_criteria: resolveMode('direct') and resolveMode('local') produce RuntimeBundles where workspace.writeFile + sandbox.exec('cat') see same content. Auto-detection: Linux+bwrap→local, else→direct.

title: Phase 1: Layout Presets (IdeLayout + ChatLayout)

## Goal Two thin preset layouts (~30 lines each) that build a LayoutConfig and pass it to DockviewShell. ## What to implement ### IdeLayout (src/layouts/IdeLayout.tsx) - Props: { sidebar?: string, center?: string, right?: string } — all panel IDs, overridable - Defaults: { sidebar: 'filetree', center: 'empty', right: undefined } - Builds LayoutConfig: - sidebar group: left, locked, collapsible (collapsedWidth: 0), constraints 200-400px - center group: center, dynamic, placeholder 'empty' - right group: optional, hideHeader, constraints minWidth 300px - Returns Full pseudo-code: ```typescript // layouts/IdeLayout.tsx — ~30 lines export function IdeLayout({ sidebar = 'filetree', center = 'empty', right }: IdeLayoutProps) { return } ``` ### ChatLayout (src/layouts/ChatLayout.tsx) - Props: { nav?: string, center?: string, surface?: string, sidebar?: string } - Defaults: { nav: 'session-list', center: 'chat', surface: undefined } - Builds LayoutConfig: - nav group: left, locked, hideHeader, fixed 60px width (minWidth=60, maxWidth=60) - center group: center - sidebar group: optional, left, collapsible (collapsedWidth: 0), constraints 200-350px - surface group: optional, right, dynamic, placeholder 'empty' - Returns - Note: surface group is where ArtifactSurfacePane renders a nested DockviewShell - ChatLayout supports an optional file tree sidebar (sidebar='filetree') Full pseudo-code: ```typescript // layouts/ChatLayout.tsx — ~30 lines export function ChatLayout({ nav = 'session-list', center = 'chat', surface, sidebar }: ChatLayoutProps) { return } ``` ### Layout types (src/layouts/types.ts) ```typescript interface IdeLayoutProps { sidebar?: string // panel ID for left sidebar (default: 'filetree') center?: string // panel ID for center area (default: 'empty') right?: string // panel ID for right rail (optional) } interface ChatLayoutProps { nav?: string // panel ID for nav rail (default: 'session-list') center?: string // panel ID for center area (default: 'chat') surface?: string // panel ID for artifact surface (optional) sidebar?: string // panel ID for optional sidebar (e.g., 'filetree') } ``` ### NavRail props contract (for ChatLayout) - { sessions, activeSessionId, onSwitch, onCreate, onNewChat, onToggleSurface, surfaceOpen } - App shell passes user menu separately (not workspace's concern) ### Shared hooks extraction (Risk 10 mitigation) Both presets use DockviewShell which handles all heavy logic internally: | Shared concern | Location | Used by | |----------------|----------|---------| | useSidebarLayout() | DockviewShell (wireCollapsible) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | | usePanelSizing() | DockviewShell (constraint enforcement) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | | usePanelConstraints() | DockviewShell (group constraints) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | v2 architecture: DockviewShell handles groups, constraints, placeholders, collapse internally. Presets are ~30 lines each building LayoutConfig. No duplicated sidebar/sizing code. ## v1 references - IdeLayout.jsx (1718 lines) — study group arrangement, simplify to ~30 lines - ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) — nav rail + chat + surface arrangement - SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) — artifact surface dockview (nested) - useDockLayout.js (337 lines) — sidebar discovery, collapse toggle ## Tier 1/2/3 composability examples ```tsx // TIER 1: Preset layout with slot overrides // TIER 2: Custom layout via DockviewShell // TIER 3: Standalone components (no dockview, no WorkspaceProvider, no context) import { FileTree, CodeEditor } from '@boring/workspace'
``` ## Considerations - Both are THIN — they only build a LayoutConfig. All heavy logic is in DockviewShell. - Apps that need custom layouts skip presets and use DockviewShell directly - Presets are not special — just convenience functions - Layout config includes version: '2.0' for persistence version tracking ## Unit tests (layouts/presets.test.ts) ### IdeLayout - Test: IdeLayout renders with default props (sidebar=filetree, center=empty) - Test: IdeLayout accepts sidebar override (custom panel ID) - Test: IdeLayout with right prop adds right rail group - Test: IdeLayout without right prop has no right rail group - Test: sidebar group is locked and collapsible - Test: center group is dynamic with placeholder 'empty' ### ChatLayout - Test: ChatLayout renders with default props (nav=session-list, center=chat) - Test: ChatLayout with surface prop adds surface group (dynamic, placeholder) - Test: ChatLayout with sidebar prop adds collapsible sidebar - Test: ChatLayout without surface or sidebar → just nav + center - Test: nav group has fixed 60px width (minWidth=maxWidth=60) - Test: nav group is locked with hidden header ### Both presets - Test: preset output is a valid LayoutConfig object - Test: all panel IDs in LayoutConfig exist in registry (integration with Risk 11 validation) - Test: presets are <50 lines each (lint check) ## Success criteria - IdeLayout renders sidebar + center + optional right rail - ChatLayout renders nav + center + optional sidebar + optional surface - All slot overrides work (custom panel IDs) - Presets are <50 lines each (thin wrappers) - Can be skipped entirely in favor of direct DockviewShell usage

title: M1: buildBwrapArgs (port — security-critical)
description:
**Goal:** Port the bwrap argument builder. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/buildBwrapArgs.ts`.

**Source:** Old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/adapters/bwrap.ts` lines ~22–58.

**Behavior:** Given `{workspaceRoot}`, returns `string[]` of `bwrap` flags creating a sandbox where:
- `/workspace` → bind-mounted from `workspaceRoot` (writable)
- Read-only binds: `/usr`, `/lib`, `/lib64`, `/bin`, `/etc/ssl`, `/etc/ca-certificates`, etc. (see `RO_BIND_DIRS`)
- `--unshare-all --share-net` (proc/user/ipc/uts isolated; keep network)
- `--die-with-parent` — so if our Node process dies, sandbox terminates.
- `--chdir /workspace`
- `--setenv HOME /workspace`
- `--proc /proc --dev /dev --tmpfs /tmp`

**Why flag order matters:** bwrap applies binds in order. Read-only binds must come BEFORE `--chdir`. Port verbatim where possible.

**Constants:**
- `BWRAP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30`
- `KILL_GRACE_SECONDS = 5`
- `RO_BIND_DIRS` — list of host paths to bind read-only.

**Tests:** Snapshot the args array for a known input; assert required flags present + ordering; assert `--bind` for workspaceRoot is present exactly once.

## Acceptance Criteria

Snapshot test for args array; required flags present; --bind for workspaceRoot present exactly once; RO binds precede --chdir.
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot test for args array; required flags present; --bind for workspaceRoot present exactly once; RO binds precede --chdir.

title: M1: createNodeWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** Node fs-backed `Workspace` adapter. File: `src/server/workspace/createNodeWorkspace.ts`.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createNodeWorkspace(root: string): Workspace {
  return {
    root,
    async readFile(rel) { const p = validatePath(root, rel); await assertRealPath...(root,p); return fs.readFile(p,'utf8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... ensureWritable ... fs.writeFile(p, data) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... fs.rm(p, { recursive: false }) },   // file OR empty dir
    async readdir(rel) { ... fs.readdir(p, { withFileTypes: true }) → Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... fs.stat(p) → Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { validate both; fs.rename(abs(from), abs(to)) },
  }
}
```

**Uses:** path helpers on every call (no exceptions).

**Shared by:** `direct` and `local` modes (both use same host fs).

**Tests:**
- Happy path for all 7 ops.
- Path traversal rejections on every op.
- `rename` within root (move file, rename file, rename dir).
- `unlink` on empty dir succeeds; on non-empty dir rejects.
- `readdir` returns `{name, kind}` — no extra fields.

**Done when:** Passes shared `Workspace` conformance suite (in infra epic).

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.

title: Phase 4: Playwright E2E Tests
description:
## Goal
Golden-path E2E tests + bridge protocol E2E test. ~2 min CI.

## What to implement

### Golden paths
- Open file → edit → save
- Resize sidebar → persist → restore
- Open panel → close panel
- Chat layout: session switch, artifact open
- Bridge: agent opens file → workspace reflects, user action → bridge event fires

### Bridge protocol E2E (~50 LOC)
- Playwright opens workspace UI
- Separate HTTP client simulates agent
- Agent POSTs openFile command → SSE event: command delivers to workspace → workspace applies locally (Zustand) → file panel appears in UI
- Tests real SSE/polling path end-to-end

### CI integration
- Run on every PR (~2 min)
- Full suite (all browsers) on merge to main

## Success criteria
- All golden paths pass
- Bridge E2E proves command flow works end-to-end
- No flaky tests
- <2 min total runtime

title: v1 Migration Context + Implementation Reference
description:
## Goal
Reference document for the implementing agent. Contains ALL v1→v2 migration context, files NOT to port, per-app compatibility, and shared knowledge the implementer needs. This bead is read-only context — no code to write, but every other bead should be understood in the context of this information.

SIMPLIFY: v1 is 1718 lines — most is panel lifecycle that belongs in hooks. |

title: M3b: /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes + tracker
description:

## Acceptance Criteria

Multiple writes+edits accumulate. Deletes recorded. Response shape stable.
