title: Phase 4: Performance Budget Enforcement
description:
## Goal
CI enforcement of bundle budgets + performance benchmarks.

## Performance budgets
| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Initial JS (shell + registry) | <150KB gz |
| Total all-loaded ceiling | <800KB gz |
| Shell render (empty layout) | <500ms TTI |
| File tree (1000 files) | <200ms render |
| First CodeMirror render | <500ms |
| First tiptap render | <500ms |
| Layout persistence round-trip | <50ms |
| Bridge command latency | <100ms (SSE), <2s (poll) |

## What to implement
- CI: vite build + chunk analysis (check two numbers: initial <150KB, total <800KB)
- Playwright performance.mark() for render timing
- Vitest benchmarks for persistence round-trip
- Fail CI if budgets exceeded

## Success criteria
- CI reports bundle sizes on every PR
- CI fails if initial >150KB or total >800KB
- Performance benchmarks run in CI

title: Phase 2: HTTP Data Provider
description:
## Goal
Typed fetch client + React Query hooks for all agent-hosted HTTP endpoints. DataProvider context so panes access data without knowing transport.

## What to implement

### DataProvider (src/providers/DataProvider.tsx)
- React context wrapping React Query client + typed fetch
- Configured via WorkspaceProvider props: apiBaseUrl, authHeaders
- Provides hooks for panes to use

### Typed fetch client
- Typed wrappers for agent-hosted endpoints:
  - GET /api/v1/tree?dir=<path> → FileEntry[]
  - GET /api/v1/files?path=<path> → { content: string, mtime: number }
  - POST /api/v1/files { path, content } → void
  - DELETE /api/v1/files?path=<path> → void
  - GET /api/v1/stat?path=<path> → { isDir, size, mtime }
  - GET /api/v1/files/search?q=<glob>&limit=<n> → string[]
- No git routes in v1 (git UI dropped)

### React Query hooks
- useFileContent(path): { data, isLoading, error, refetch }
- useFileList(dir): { data, isLoading, error } — with refetchInterval: 3000ms
- useFileWrite(): mutation hook for POST /api/v1/files
- useFileSearch(query, limit): search hook with debounce
- useStat(path): file metadata

### Error handling
- 401 → fire onAuthError callback (app shell handles redirect)
- 404 → file not found UI (component-level)
- 5xx → auto-retry 3x with exponential backoff
- Timeout: 10s default

## v1 references
- queries.js (10KB) — React Query hooks, query key patterns
- httpProvider.js (8.4KB) — HTTP client, auth header injection, 401 retry
- DROP: useGitStatus, lightningFs, isomorphicGit, pyodide providers

## Considerations
- Data provider ONLY used by pane wrappers (FileTreePane, CodeEditorPane, etc.)
- Standalone components (FileTree, CodeEditor) accept all data via props — NO DataProvider needed
- DataProvider is a React context wrapper for convenience, NOT an abstraction for swappable backends
- Polling paused during active edits (refetchInterval disabled when dirty)

## Success criteria
- All hooks work with mock API responses
- Error handling: 401 fires callback, 5xx retries, timeout shows toast
- Query keys properly scoped (path-based cache invalidation)
- Hooks usable inside WorkspaceProvider context

title: M2: E2E — mode flip works with zero code changes
description:
**Goal:** Prove the architectural claim. `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same UI, same tools; agent runs in a Firecracker VM instead of host/bwrap.

**Test approach (Playwright against a real or mocked Vercel account):**
1. Boot with `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox`.
2. Send "list files in /" via chat.
3. Assert response mentions `/vercel/sandbox` or similar VM paths, NOT host paths.
4. Write a file via the UI's FileTree "new file" action.
5. Send "cat the new file" → assert contents match.

**Logging:** heavy test logs tagged `[m2-modeflip]` for CI debugging. Print sandbox ID, API call counts, cold-start duration.

**Done when:** flipping env vars works both ways; no code changes needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` works same as local. Flipping env vars both ways works. No code changes.
acceptance_criteria: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` works same as local. Flipping env vars both ways works. No code changes.

title: Deferred: GitHub Connect + /api/v1/git/* routes (v1.x)
description:
**Scope:** Git UI dropped from agent v1 AND workspace v1. Neither package has a git consumer → `/api/v1/git/*` routes would be dead code. Agent runs git via the `bash` tool (`git status`, `git commit`, etc.).

**Design already documented (for future):**
- Git routes ~200 LOC of thin `sandbox.exec('git …')` wrappers with output parsing.
- Credentials in remote mode: token-in-URL for HTTPS (`https://x-access-token:$TOKEN@github.com/…`).
- SSE invalidation: `data-git-changed` events after write ops.
- VM persistence: git config (`user.name`, `user.email`) seeded on sandbox create.

**Trigger to implement:** any UI consumer emerges (agent v1.x status bar, or workspace reviving git badges).

**Acceptance:** stays deferred until consumer lands; design notes preserved in this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria

Stays deferred until UI consumer lands. Design notes preserved in this bead.

description:
## Goal
Mobile sidebar as sheet, tablet auto-collapse, minimum panel sizes enforced.

## What to implement

title: Risk: Multi-tab concurrency (document + observe)
description:
**Risk class:** Accepted risk (spec: silent last-write-wins on multi-tab file writes).

**Scope for v1:** DOCUMENT, don't mitigate.

**Deliverable:**
- `docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md` entry: "Opening two tabs and editing the same file simultaneously: the later write wins silently. The agent has no awareness of concurrent edits."
- Future mitigation seam: optimistic write with `If-Match` header + 409 on conflict.

**Why accept:** dev-tool posture; mitigations cost >> user exposure for v1.

**Trigger to re-evaluate:** first bug report where a user loses edits.

## Acceptance Criteria

Documented in `docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md`. Future mitigation seam identified.

title: M1: editTool factory (string-replace)
description:
**Goal:** `edit` tool — string-replace edits on existing files. File: `src/server/catalog/tools/editTool.ts`.

**Parameters:** `{ path: string, oldString: string, newString: string, replaceAll?: boolean }`.

**Behavior:**
- Reads file via workspace.readFile.
- If `oldString` not present → error (agent must use exact content).
- If `replaceAll: false` and `oldString` appears more than once → error ("ambiguous; add context or replaceAll").
- Replaces + writes back via workspace.writeFile.
- Returns `{ path, replacements: number }`.

**Tests:** unique match replace; ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll; replaceAll succeeds; no-match rejects.

**Why NOT a diff-based edit tool:** Claude Code pattern. Exact-string-replace is simpler for LLMs than unified-diff; fewer parse errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

Unique-match replace works. Ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll. replaceAll succeeds on multiples. No-match rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Unique-match replace works. Ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll. replaceAll succeeds on multiples. No-match rejects.

description:

title: M3b: Tool renderers — ai-elements generic + DiffView for edit
description:
**Goal:** Render bash/read/write with ai-elements generic `Tool` + `Terminal` + `CodeBlock`. Only one custom renderer: `DiffView` for `edit` tool. Files: `src/front/toolRenderers/*`.

**Mapping:**
- `bash` → `<Tool>{<Terminal>{stdout, stderr}</Terminal>}</Tool>` (stdin=command)
- `read` → `<Tool>{<CodeBlock>{content}</CodeBlock>}</Tool>`
- `write` → `<Tool>` with summary `Wrote N bytes to path` (no content render — it's on disk)
- `edit` → `<DiffView oldString={input.oldString} newString={input.newString} path={input.path} />` — custom unified-diff UI.

**DiffView reference:** Port + adapt `packages/agent/src/front/components/chat/EditToolRenderer.jsx` from old boring-ui.

**Override seam:** `<ChatPanel toolRenderers={{ myCustomTool: MyRenderer }} />`. Default map inlined; override merges deep.

**Tests:** each renderer snapshot; DiffView renders multi-line changes cleanly; override swaps correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each built-in tool has a renderer snapshot. DiffView renders multi-line changes cleanly. Override via `toolRenderers` prop merges deep.
acceptance_criteria: Each built-in tool has a renderer snapshot. DiffView renders multi-line changes cleanly. Override via `toolRenderers` prop merges deep.

title: Research: Tailwind v4 conflict with workspace styles
description:
**Question:** When `@boring/workspace` also uses Tailwind v4, do our utility classes and theirs compose cleanly, or do we get preflight resets conflicting with the workspace's global styles?

**Investigation:**
1. Set up a minimal workspace-like host that imports both packages.
2. Render ChatPanel inside a workspace layout that also has Tailwind v4.
3. Check DevTools for specificity conflicts, unexpected resets, class-name collisions.

**Expected issues:**
- Two preflight injections → double-reset.
- Scoped utility classes bleed.

**Mitigation candidates (pick one):**
- Use CSS Modules inside primitives (bypass Tailwind for agent-internal styling).
- Ship a scoping config (prefix `agt-` on all our classes).
- Document "don't enable Tailwind preflight in both packages" as a hard requirement.

**Acceptance:** integration test or documented constraint.

## Acceptance Criteria

Integration test OR documented 'don't enable preflight in both packages' constraint.

title: M1: Build pipeline (tsup + vite)
description:
**Goal:** `pnpm build` produces shippable artifacts. Files: `tsup.config.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, `package.json` scripts.

**Outputs:**
- `dist/shared/index.js` + `.d.ts` (pure types + interfaces)
- `dist/server/index.js` + `.d.ts` (Node-only entrypoints)
- `dist/front/index.js` + `.d.ts` (React components + hooks)
- `dist/frontend/` (Vite-built SPA; served statically by CLI)
- `dist/bin/boring-agent.js` (bundled CLI with shebang)

**package.json:**
- `"bin": { "boring-agent": "./dist/bin/boring-agent.js" }`
- `"exports"`: `.` / `./shared` / `./server` / `./front`

**Tests:** CI runs `pnpm build` and asserts all dist outputs exist + parseable.

## Acceptance Criteria

CI runs `pnpm build` and asserts all dist outputs exist + parseable. `bin` field points at compiled CLI.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs `pnpm build` and asserts all dist outputs exist + parseable. `bin` field points at compiled CLI.

title: Research: Plugin tool name collision precedence
description:
**Question:** When a pi plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, who wins?

**Investigation:**
1. Write a synthetic plugin that registers a tool named `bash` with behavior "return 'FROM PLUGIN'".
2. Load standard catalog + plugin → invoke `bash` via chat → observe output.
3. Vary registration order: ours-then-plugin vs plugin-then-ours.
4. Try pi's namespace mechanisms if any.

**Document:** exact precedence rule in `docs/PLUGINS.md`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins (plugins after defaults = plugin wins).

**Future:** namespace defaults as `boring.bash` — deferred unless collision reports pile up.

**Acceptance:** rule documented with reproducible test.

## Acceptance Criteria

Precedence rule documented with reproducible test.

title: Phase 3a: Agent Pane Slot
description:
## Goal
Register ChatPanel from @boring/agent as a panel in the workspace registry. Workspace does NOT import from agent — the app shell passes the component in.

## What to implement
- ChatPanel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop (app shell provides it)
- Default placement: 'right' (IdeLayout) or center slot (ChatLayout)
- Lazy-loaded with React.lazy + Suspense wrapper
- requiresCapabilities: ['agent.chat'] — only available when capability is set

## How it works (no circular dependency)

- App shell passes it to WorkspaceProvider panels prop

title: Phase 3a: Chat Layout Integration
description:
## Goal
Integrate ChatLayout with agent pane, artifact surface (nested DockviewShell), session list UI, and chat hooks.

## What to implement

### ArtifactSurfacePane (src/panes/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx)
- Renders its own nested DockviewShell inside ChatLayout's surface group
- storageKey: 'boring-ui-v2:surface' (own persistence, independent of outer shell)
- allowedPanels: ['code-editor', 'markdown-editor', 'csv-viewer', 'empty'] (guard against outer-shell panels)
- Only rendered when artifacts exist (conditional mount)
- Sync suppression flag prevents feedback loops between outer and inner state

Nested shell pseudo-code (from plan Section E):
```typescript
// panes/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx — renders inside ChatLayout's surface group
function ArtifactSurfacePane({ artifacts, onSelectArtifact, onCloseArtifact }) {
  return (
    <DockviewShell
      layout={{
        groups: [{ id: 'artifacts', position: 'center', dynamic: true, placeholder: 'empty' }]
      }}
      storageKey="boring-ui-v2:surface"    // own persistence, independent of outer shell
      allowedPanels={['code-editor', 'markdown-editor', 'csv-viewer', 'empty']}
    />
  )
}
// Filters outer panel registry to allowed IDs. Has own API, own state, own lifecycle.
```

### Nested DockviewShell — isolation model (ninth pass)
Nesting works naturally because dockview instances are independent. No formal isolation protocol needed for v2.

1. **Persistence**: Each DockviewShell with unique storageKey creates its own Zustand persist partition. Root uses boring-ui-v2:layout, nested surface uses boring-ui-v2:surface.
2. **API scoping**: useDockviewApi() returns the API for the nearest ancestor DockviewShell. Panes inside nested shell interact with the correct instance.
3. **No panel ID namespacing**: panel IDs are plain strings. If two shells have panels with same ID, they're independent (different dockview instances).
4. **No bridge routing**: bridge commands always target root shell. Nested shell panels managed directly by the component rendering the nested DockviewShell.

### Chat hooks (simplified from v1's 8 → 2)
- **useArtifactPanels()** (~50 lines): open/close/track artifact panels in surface dockview
- **useArtifactRouting()** (~30 lines): optional convenience mapping tool names to panel types

**Eliminated by bridge** (no longer needed — these were v1 coupling mechanisms):
| v1 hook | Replacement |
|---------|-------------|
| useShellStatePublisher | → store subscriptions |
| useShellPersistence | → Zustand persist |
| useSessionState/Store/ServerSessions | → agent owns via bridge props |
| useToolBridge | → agent calls bridge.openPanel() explicitly |

### Session list UI (workspace renders, agent owns state)

Session list ownership boundary (from plan Resolved Questions):
```
@boring/agent (owns state):
  sessions: Session[]
  activeSessionId: string
  switchSession(id: string): void
  createSession(): Session
  deleteSession(id: string): void

@boring/workspace (owns UI):
  <SessionList
    sessions={agent.sessions}
    activeId={agent.activeSessionId}
    onSwitch={agent.switchSession}
    onCreate={agent.createSession}
    onDelete={agent.deleteSession}
  />
```

- SessionList component: renders { id, title, updatedAt }[] from agent
- Calls agent's switchSession/createSession/deleteSession
- Active session indicator
- Context menu: rename, delete
- Workspace doesn't know what a "session" contains — just renders the list with titles and handles the chrome

### NavRail integration
- NavRail props: { sessions, activeSessionId, onSwitch, onCreate, onNewChat, onToggleSurface, surfaceOpen }
- App shell passes user menu separately (not workspace's concern)

### Tool → artifact routing simplification
v1 approach: toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4KB) + useToolBridge.js (5.3KB) — workspace infers panel type from tool results.
v2 approach: agent explicitly calls bridge.openPanel() — workspace no longer infers. useArtifactRouting() is an optional convenience (~30 lines) that maps common tool names to panel types for backward compatibility.

## v1 references
- ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) — nav rail + chat + surface
- SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) — artifact surface dockview (nested)
- NavRail.jsx (133 lines) — session switcher
- toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4KB) + useToolBridge.js (5.3KB) — tool→artifact routing (simplified in v2)
- SurfaceDockview pattern: filtered registry via allowedPanels, gets layout via props, uses callbacks for state flow

## Considerations
- Nested DockviewShell: independent instance, own API, own state, own lifecycle
- useDockviewApi() inside nested shell returns nested shell's API (nearest ancestor)
- No panel ID namespacing needed (different dockview instances)
- Bridge commands target root shell only (no multi-shell routing in v1)
- If multi-shell routing needed post-launch (agent opening artifacts in surface from outside), add shell parameter to bridge commands then

## Unit tests (layouts/chat-integration.test.tsx)

### ArtifactSurfacePane
- Test: renders nested DockviewShell with storageKey="boring-ui-v2:surface"
- Test: allowedPanels filters registry (only code-editor, markdown-editor, csv-viewer, empty)
- Test: outer-shell panel IDs not available in nested shell
- Test: conditional mount — not rendered when no artifacts
- Test: sync suppression — inner state changes don't feedback to outer

### Chat hooks
- Test: useArtifactPanels() opens artifact panel in surface dockview
- Test: useArtifactPanels() closes artifact panel
- Test: useArtifactPanels() tracks open artifact panels
- Test: useArtifactRouting() maps tool name to correct panel type
- Test: useArtifactRouting() returns undefined for unknown tool

### Session list
- Test: SessionList renders list of { id, title, updatedAt }
- Test: clicking session calls onSwitch(id)
- Test: create button calls onCreate
- Test: context menu delete calls onDelete(id)
- Test: active session has indicator
- Test: empty session list shows "No sessions" state

### NavRail
- Test: NavRail renders with sessions list
- Test: onNewChat callback fires on button click
- Test: onToggleSurface toggles surface open state
- Test: surfaceOpen prop reflects in toggle button state

### Nested shell independence
- Test: useDockviewApi() inside nested shell returns nested shell's API
- Test: bridge commands target root shell, not nested
- Test: nested shell has independent persistence (different storageKey)

## E2E tests (Playwright)
- Test: ChatLayout session switch → correct session displayed
- Test: ChatLayout artifact open → surface appears with correct content
- Test: agent message in chat → artifact renders in surface
- Test: nested shell resize independent of outer shell

## Success criteria
- ChatLayout renders with agent chat in center, artifacts in surface
- Artifact surface opens/closes correctly (conditional mount)
- Session list displays and switches sessions
- Nested DockviewShell has independent persistence
- allowedPanels guard prevents outer panels from rendering in surface
- Two chat hooks work correctly (artifact panels + routing)

title: M0: Interface — Sandbox + SandboxCapability + ExecResult
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 sandbox contract. File: `src/shared/sandbox.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export type SandboxCapability = 'exec' | 'isolated-code'

export interface Sandbox {
  readonly id: string
  readonly placement: 'server' | 'browser'
  readonly capabilities: readonly SandboxCapability[]
  init(ctx: { workspace: Workspace; sessionId: string }): Promise<void>
  exec(cmd: string, opts?: ExecOptions): Promise<ExecResult>
  executeIsolatedCode?(input: IsolatedCodeInput): Promise<IsolatedCodeOutput>
  dispose?(): Promise<void>
}

export interface ExecOptions {
  timeoutMs?: number                  // default 30_000 — safety cap
  maxOutputBytes?: number             // default 1_048_576 — safety cap
  env?: Record<string, string>
  cwd?: string
  stdin?: string
}

export interface ExecResult {
  stdout: Uint8Array                  // RAW BYTES, binary-safe — server adapter decodes per encoding
  stderr: Uint8Array
  exitCode: number
  durationMs: number
  truncated: boolean
  stdoutEncoding?: 'utf-8' | 'binary'
  stderrEncoding?: 'utf-8' | 'binary'
}
```

**Invariants:**
- `Uint8Array` (NOT `Buffer` — Node-only type leak forbidden in shared contracts).
- Every impl enforces `timeoutMs` and `maxOutputBytes` — bytes beyond cap get dropped and `truncated: true`.
- `capabilities` declares what this sandbox supports. v1 sandboxes declare `['exec']`. Future ones add `'isolated-code'` which unlocks the 5th catalog tool.

**Tests:** Type-level stub + conformance-test requirement noted for infra.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/sandbox.ts` exports `Sandbox`, `SandboxCapability`, `ExecOptions`, `ExecResult`. `stdout`/`stderr` are `Uint8Array` (not `Buffer`). Type-level stub passes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/sandbox.ts` exports `Sandbox`, `SandboxCapability`, `ExecOptions`, `ExecResult`. `stdout`/`stderr` are `Uint8Array` (not `Buffer`). Type-level stub passes.

title: M1: createPiCodingAgentHarness
description:
**Goal:** Wrap `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` as our `AgentHarness`. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/createHarness.ts`.

**Reference:** Old `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/runtime.ts` — IMPORTANT the old code uses `pi-agent-core`; v2 uses `pi-coding-agent` (which wraps pi-agent-core + plugins + sessions). API surface differs.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createPiCodingAgentHarness(opts: { tools: AgentTool[] }): AgentHarness {
  return {
    id: 'pi-coding-agent', placement: 'server',
    sessions: new PiSessionStore(),   // in-memory in M1; JSONL-backed in M3a
    async *sendMessage(input, ctx) {
      const session = createAgentSession({ tools: opts.tools })   // pi API
      const piStream = session.runTurn({
        sessionId: input.sessionId, message: input.message,
        model: input.model, thinkingLevel: input.thinkingLevel,
        abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal,
      })
      yield* piEventsToUIMessageChunks(piStream)                   // see m1.stream-adapter
    },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- **Pi built-ins are skipped.** We pass only our `tools: [...]`. Verified by M1 acceptance test (`m1.test.pi-builtin-opt-out`).
- TypeBox → JSON Schema adaptation at the tool boundary: our tools declare JSON Schema; pi receives TypeBox; adapter converts one-way.
- Model registry adaptation: our `'sonnet' | 'haiku' | 'opus'` → pi's model IDs.

**Tests:** one round-trip: send a message with a no-op tool, assert at least one chunk arrives; assert no pi default tool (like pi's bash) is registered.

## Acceptance Criteria

createAgentSession receives our tools array only; no pi built-ins in catalog (verified by `research.pi-builtin-optout`). Single turn roundtrip works.
acceptance_criteria: createAgentSession receives our tools array only; no pi built-ins in catalog (verified by `research.pi-builtin-optout`). Single turn roundtrip works.

title: M1: Path helpers + realpath validation (port)
description:
**Goal:** Port + adapt the path validation primitives. File: `src/server/workspace/paths.ts`.

**Functions to port (from old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/workspace/paths.ts` ~80 LOC):**
- `validatePath(workspaceRoot, relPath): string` — rejects `..`, absolute paths, null bytes; returns joined absolute path.
- `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace(workspaceRoot, absPath): Promise<void>` — `realpath()` on the resolved path; rejects if symlink escapes root.
- `ensureExistingWorkspacePath(workspaceRoot, relPath)` — validate + realpath + existence check.
- `ensureWritableWorkspacePath(workspaceRoot, relPath)` — validate + dir-exists check on parent.

**Why port, not rewrite:** battle-hardened, already fuzzed in production. Copy verbatim where possible; only adapt imports.

**Security invariants:**
- Rejects `..` segments before resolve.
- Rejects absolute paths.
- Rejects null bytes.
- Follows symlinks via `realpath` and checks final resolved path starts with `realpath(workspaceRoot)`.

**Tests:** path traversal attack corpus (~20 vectors: `../../etc/passwd`, `foo/../../../bar`, `/etc/passwd`, `foo\x00bar`, symlink-to-outside). Every vector must throw.

**Logging:** on reject, log the vector + reason (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`) for debugging forensics.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 20+ path-traversal vectors in `infra.path-traversal-corpus` rejected. Helpers throw with categorized reason codes (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`).

title: M3a: Stream resumption — ring buffer + cursor route
description:
**Goal:** Survivable streaming across client disconnects. Files: `src/server/http/routes/chat.ts` (extended), `src/server/http/streamBuffer.ts`.

**Architecture:**
- **Transport-owned.** Harness has no `reconnect?()`. Any future harness inherits resume automatically.
- Per `(sessionId, turnId)`, keep an **in-memory ring buffer** of emitted chunks + monotonic `chunkIdx`.

**Ring buffer details:**
- Max chunks per buffer: 2000 (tuneable). Older chunks evicted; resume requests for evicted cursors get a 'replay from persistent store' fallback.
- TTL: 60s idle after turn completion. Dropped thereafter.
- Memory bound: ~2000 chunks × ~1KB avg = ~2MB per active turn. Acceptable for v1.

**Flow:**
1. On `POST /api/v1/agent/chat`: allocate buffer, stream chunks to client AND append to buffer.
2. On turn complete: mark buffer complete, hold for TTL, then GC.
3. On `GET /api/v1/agent/chat/:sessionId/:turnId?cursor=<n>`:
   - If buffer exists + turn in-flight: replay chunks `> cursor`, continue live stream.
   - If buffer exists + turn complete: replay from buffer, close.
   - If buffer evicted: fall back to `PiSessionStore.load(sessionId)` + serialize from `UIMessage[]` (lossy — no exact chunk-level replay, but semantically correct).
   - If turn not found: 404.

**Client-side (useChat):**
- Wired with `experimental_resume: true`.
- On page reload mid-stream: useChat calls the resume endpoint with last known cursor.

**Tests:**
- Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect → receives chunks `> cursor` only (no duplicates).
- Completed turn replay returns full transcript.
- Buffer eviction: stale buffers dropped after 60s (assert memory release).
- Evicted buffer resume: serves from SessionStore fallback.
- Cursor > buffer length → 416 Range Not Satisfiable.

**Logging:** `[resume]` prefix; log cursor + buffer size + eviction events.

## Acceptance Criteria

Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect receives chunks > cursor only (no duplicates). Completed replay returns full transcript. Buffer eviction after 60s. Evicted buffer falls back to SessionStore. Cursor > buffer → 416.
acceptance_criteria: Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect receives chunks > cursor only (no duplicates). Completed replay returns full transcript. Buffer eviction after 60s. Evicted buffer falls back to SessionStore. Cursor > buffer → 416.

title: M3a: Abort propagation (useChat.stop → pi → sandbox.exec)
description:
**Goal:** `useChat.stop()` cleanly halts the full pipeline. Files: chat route + harness + sandbox adapters.

**Chain:**
1. Client `stop()` → fetch abort.
2. Route's AbortController signals.
3. Passes to `harness.sendMessage(..., {abortSignal})`.
4. Harness passes to pi's `runTurn({abortSignal})`.
5. Pi propagates to any in-flight `tool.execute({abortSignal})`.
6. Our tools pass to `sandbox.exec({abortSignal})`.
7. Sandbox adapter kills the process (bwrap) or aborts the Vercel runCommand.

**Accepted risk:** pi's abort propagation is unproven for all cases. Test each mode in M1 and M2.

**Tests:** mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end; assert no orphaned bwrap child processes; assert sandbox.exec promise rejects with AbortError.

## Acceptance Criteria

Mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end. No orphaned bwrap child processes. sandbox.exec rejects with AbortError.
acceptance_criteria: Mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end. No orphaned bwrap child processes. sandbox.exec rejects with AbortError.

title: Phase 3a: Workspace Bridge — State Store + Commands
description:
## Goal
WorkspaceBridge implementation: command dispatcher, typed event system, selector-based subscriptions, usePanelActions hook, and bridge command validation. Bridge reads/writes the single Zustand store.

## Full Type Definitions

### WorkspaceBridge interface
\`\`\`typescript
interface WorkspaceBridge {
  // Read (agent sees what user sees)
  getOpenPanels(): PanelState[]
  getActiveFile(): string | null
  getDirtyFiles(): string[]
  getVisibleFiles(): string[]

  // Write (agent acts like a user) — all return Promise<CommandResult>
  openFile(path: string, opts?: { mode?: 'view' | 'edit' | 'diff' }): Promise<CommandResult>
  openPanel(config: DynamicPaneConfig): Promise<CommandResult>
  closePanel(id: string): Promise<CommandResult>
  expandToFile(path: string): Promise<CommandResult>
  showNotification(msg: string, level?: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'): Promise<CommandResult>
  navigateToLine(file: string, line: number): Promise<CommandResult>
  markDirty(path: string): void
  markClean(path: string): void

  // Subscribe — typed events with discriminated union
  subscribe<K extends keyof BridgeEventMap>(
    event: K,
    handler: (data: BridgeEventMap[K]) => void
  ): Unsubscribe

  // Selector-based subscription (fires only when selected slice changes)
  select<T>(selector: (state: WorkspaceState) => T, handler: (value: T) => void): Unsubscribe

  // Connectivity
  onDisconnect(handler: () => void): Unsubscribe
  onReconnect(handler: () => void): Unsubscribe
}
\`\`\`

### CommandResult
\`\`\`typescript
interface CommandResult {
  seq: number
  status: 'ok' | 'error'
  error?: { code: string, message: string }
}
\`\`\`

### BridgeEventMap (exhaustive, no stringly typing)
\`\`\`typescript
interface BridgeEventMap {
  'panel:opened': { panelId: string; params: Record<string, unknown> }
  'panel:closed': { panelId: string }
  'panel:activated': { panelId: string; previousPanelId: string | null }
  'file:opened': { path: string; mode: 'view' | 'edit' | 'diff' }
  'file:saved': { path: string }
  'file:dirty': { path: string; dirty: boolean }
  'sidebar:toggled': { collapsed: boolean }
  'notification:shown': { message: string; level: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error' }
  'pane:error': { panelId: string; error: string; stack?: string }
}
// Apps extend via module augmentation:
// declare module '@boring/workspace' {
//   interface BridgeEventMap { 'myapp:custom': { payload: string } }
// }
\`\`\`

### UIStatePut (PUT body format)
\`\`\`typescript
interface UIStatePut {
  v: 1                                    // protocol version
  causedBy: 'user' | 'agent' | 'restore' // prevents agent echo loops
  openPanels: PanelState[]
  activePanel: string | null
  activeFile: string | null
  visibleFiles: string[]                  // file paths shown in tree
  dirtyFiles: string[]                    // files with unsaved changes
}
\`\`\`

## What to implement

### bridge/commands.ts — command definitions + dispatcher
- Command definitions with per-kind dispatch:
  - openFile(path, opts?) → file-type routing via PanelRegistry.resolve(), then addPanel via DockviewShellApi
  - openPanel(config) → addPanel with explicit component. Idempotent: duplicate ID activates existing (prefer_existing: true)
  - closePanel(id) → removePanel. Reject if essential flag is set.
  - showNotification(msg, level) → add to notifications slice in store
  - navigateToLine(file, line) → openFile + set cursor position via panel params
  - expandToFile(path) → expand tree to path, scroll to file
  - markDirty(path) / markClean(path) → update dirtyFiles map in store
- All write commands return Promise<CommandResult>
- Fire-and-forget works (just don't await)
- Max 50 open panels per workspace (hard cap, not rate)

### bridge/useWorkspaceBridge.ts — hook for panes
- Returns WorkspaceBridge interface
- Backed by the single Zustand store
- Local commands (from user) → immediate Zustand update + PUT state
- Agent commands (from SSE) → same Zustand update code path
- causedBy field distinguishes source on PUT

### hooks/usePanelActions.ts — exported public hook
- open(path): resolve file type → open in correct editor, activate panel
- close(panelId): close panel, activate next tab
- activate(panelId): bring panel to front
- openToSide(path): open file in a new panel beside current
- Thin wrapper around DockviewShellApi + bridge methods
- Extracted from v1's usePanelActions.js (16 KB). v2 is much smaller — only core open/close/activate. File-specific logic stays in panes.

### Bridge State Scope
- Bridge sends: openPanels, activePanel, activeFile, visibleFiles (file tree paths), dirtyFiles
- Bridge does NOT send: full file tree state (agent queries files via its own tools)
- This eliminates bandwidth problem of broadcasting 10K+ entries on every panel change

### dirtyFiles slice
- Map<string, { panelId: string, savedAt: number | null }>
- Updated by editors via bridge.markDirty/markClean
- Exposed to agents via bridge.getDirtyFiles()
- Enables auto-save-before-commit workflows

## SECURITY-CRITICAL: Bridge Command Validation

Every bridge command passes through a per-kind Zod validator before execution. Unknown kinds are rejected. This runs client-side on SSE commands AND is enforced server-side by @boring/agent.

| Kind | Params | Constraints |
|------|--------|-------------|
| openFile | path: string, mode?: 'view'\|'edit'\|'diff' | path max 1024 chars, no path traversal (../) |
| openPanel | id: string, component: string, params?: Record<string, JsonSerializable> | id alphanum+dash max 64, component must exist in registry, params max 16KB |
| closePanel | id: string | must be open, must NOT be essential |
| showNotification | msg: string, level?: 'info'\|'warn'\|'error' | msg max 500 chars, plain text only (NO HTML) |
| navigateToLine | file: string, line: number | file path validated same as openFile, line positive integer |
| expandToFile | path: string | path validated same as openFile |

### Path parameter safety
openFile, navigateToLine, expandToFile paths MUST NOT shortcircuit to direct file reads. All path params go through same validation as agent-side fileRoutes.

### Text parameter safety
showNotification msg, panel titles: rendered as React text nodes ({msg}), NEVER via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Truncated to max length.

### Rate limiting
Deferred to post-launch. Bridge commands are infrequent (~1 per agent turn). Max 50 open panels hard cap enforced.

## Risk 14: Bridge command lifecycle (async but returns Promise)
bridge.openFile('/file.ts') is async internally (needs file-type resolution + DockviewShellApi.addPanel). Agent calls openFile then immediately reads getOpenPanels() — file isn't there yet.

Mitigation: All commands return Promise<CommandResult>. Agent can await:
\`\`\`typescript
const { seq, status } = await bridge.openFile('/file.ts')
const panels = bridge.getOpenPanels()  // now includes the file
\`\`\`

## v1 References
- frontendState.js (2.6 KB) — current UI state bridge: panel snapshots, command queuing
- uiStateImpl.ts (201 lines) — state shape: { openPanels, activeFile, commands }
- usePanelActions.js (16 KB) — open/close/activate, file-to-panel routing

## Success Criteria
- All bridge commands work and return CommandResult
- Events fire for all state changes (each event type tested)
- Selector subscriptions only fire when selected slice changes
- dirtyFiles accurately tracks unsaved changes
- Module augmentation works for custom events
- usePanelActions hook: open, close, activate work correctly
- Validation rejects invalid commands (bad paths, essential panels, oversized params)
- Text params rendered safely (no XSS)
- Max 50 panels enforced
- causedBy correctly set on PUT body for user vs agent vs restore

## Unit Tests
- Each bridge command: happy path + error path (bad params, essential, max panels)
- BridgeEventMap: each event type fires correctly
- select() subscription: fires on slice change, NOT on unrelated changes
- dirtyFiles: markDirty adds entry, markClean removes, getDirtyFiles returns correct list
- usePanelActions: open resolves file type correctly, close activates next tab
- Zod validation: each command kind validated with correct constraints
- Path validation: ../ rejected, max length enforced
- Text validation: >500 chars truncated, HTML not interpreted
- causedBy: user actions get 'user', SSE commands get 'agent', restore gets 'restore'
- Idempotency: openPanel with duplicate ID activates existing (no second panel)

title: M1: Pi event → UIMessage stream adapter
description:
**Goal:** Convert pi `SessionEntry` events to AI SDK `UIMessageChunk` stream. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/streamAdapter.ts`.

**Reference:** Old `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/canonicalStream.ts` — study event-emission pattern but REWRITE for `createUIMessageStream` (not raw SSE).

**Full event mapping table (pi → AI SDK):**

| Pi SessionEntry kind | AI SDK UIMessageChunk | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `text-start` | `{type:'text-start', id}` | |
| `text-delta` | `{type:'text-delta', id, delta}` | streamed content |
| `text-end` | `{type:'text-end', id}` | |
| `reasoning-start` | `{type:'reasoning-start', id}` | thinking blocks |
| `reasoning-delta` | `{type:'reasoning-delta', id, delta}` | |
| `reasoning-end` | `{type:'reasoning-end', id}` | |
| `tool-input-start` | `{type:'tool-input-start', toolCallId, toolName}` | |
| `tool-input-delta` | `{type:'tool-input-delta', toolCallId, delta}` | |
| `tool-input-available` | `{type:'tool-input-available', toolCallId, input}` | complete input |
| `tool-output-available` | `{type:'tool-output-available', toolCallId, output}` | |
| `tool-output-error` | `{type:'tool-output-error', toolCallId, errorText}` | |
| `finish` | `{type:'finish'}` | |
| `abort` | `{type:'error', errorText:'Aborted'}` + `{type:'finish'}` | convert abort to clean finish |
| `usage` | `{type:'data-usage', data:{input,output,cost}}` | for /cost slash command |
| `*` (unknown) | `{type:'data-status', data:{level:'warn', msg:'unknown pi event', original}}` | future-compat |

**Heartbeat (M3b):** no-op here; injected upstream by the route (see `m3b.heartbeat-events`).

**Tests:**
- Conformance fixture: given a scripted pi event stream, assert exact UIMessageChunk sequence.
- Per-event-type round-trip: every mapping in the table has a unit test.
- Unknown/future pi event types are passed through as `data-status` so they render as warnings rather than crash.
- Abort events produce clean UI (no 'Error: ...' stack in chat; just a clean 'aborted' status).

**Logging:** `[stream-adapter]` prefix; log each unknown event type with sample data for forensics.

**Dependencies:** blocked by `m1.pi-harness`.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every pi event type in the mapping table has a unit test. Unknown event types become `data-status` warnings, not crashes. Abort events map to clean finish.
acceptance_criteria: Every pi event type in the mapping table has a unit test. Unknown event types become `data-status` warnings, not crashes. Abort events map to clean finish.

title: Phase 1: Panel Registry + Command Registry
description:
## Goal
Panel registration system (register/get/list/resolve panels by ID or file pattern) and separate CommandRegistry for command palette commands.

## What to implement

### PanelRegistry (src/registry/PanelRegistry.ts)
- register(id, config: PanelConfig): register a panel
- get(id): get panel config by ID
- list(): all registered panels
- has(id): check existence
- resolve(filename): file-type routing — match filename against filePatterns
- getComponents(): returns component map for DockviewReact (lazy-loaded via React.lazy)

### File-type routing precedence (Risk 13)
Explicit precedence rules:
1. App-registered panels checked before built-ins
2. Longest suffix match wins (*.test.ts > *.ts > *)
3. Tied: first registered wins (deterministic — registration order)
4. bridge.openFile(path, { panel: 'code-editor' }) with explicit panel bypasses routing

Implementation: resolve(filename) iterates panels in registration order (app first, then built-in). For each panel with filePatterns, test each glob against filename. Track best match by suffix length. Return panel with longest suffix match.

### Built-in panels (all lazy-loaded except empty)
- filetree: () => import('./panes/FileTreePane') (~15KB gz with react-arborist)
- code-editor: () => import('./panes/CodeEditorPane') (~250KB gz)
- markdown-editor: () => import('./panes/MarkdownEditorPane') (~300KB gz)
- data-catalog: () => import('./panes/DataCatalogPane') (~2KB gz)
- empty: eagerly loaded (<1KB)

### Default file-type routes
- *.md, *.mdx → markdown-editor
- * (fallback) → code-editor

### Capability gating
- PanelConfig.requiresCapabilities?: string[]
- Registry filters available panels based on WorkspaceProvider.capabilities prop
- e.g., agent panel requires 'agent.chat' capability
- list() and getComponents() only return panels whose capabilities are satisfied

### CommandRegistry (src/registry/CommandRegistry.ts) — tenth pass, separate from panels
- registerCommand(config: CommandConfig): register a command
- getCommands(): all registered commands
- getCommand(id): get by ID
- CommandConfig { id: string, title: string, run: () => void, shortcut?: string, when?: () => boolean }
- Built-in commands: Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Agent Panel, Save File, Close Tab
- Used by CommandPalette (Phase 4) with > prefix
- Panels and extensions register custom commands at runtime

### RegistryProvider (src/registry/RegistryProvider.tsx)
- React context providing both PanelRegistry and CommandRegistry
- useRegistry() hook for panels
- useCommandRegistry() hook for commands

### Types (src/registry/types.ts)

```typescript
interface PanelConfig {
  id: string
  component: React.ComponentType<PaneProps> | (() => Promise<{ default: React.ComponentType<PaneProps> }>)
  title?: string
  icon?: React.ComponentType<{ size: number }>  // lucide-react compatible
  placement?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right' | 'bottom'
  essential?: boolean  // cannot be closed by user
  hideHeader?: boolean
  requiresCapabilities?: string[]
  filePatterns?: string[]  // file globs this pane handles (e.g., ['*.csv', '*.parquet'])

  // Lifecycle hooks — called by DockviewShell, run outside React render cycle
  onActivate?: (api: PanelLifecycleApi) => void
  onDeactivate?: (api: PanelLifecycleApi) => void
  onClose?: (api: PanelLifecycleApi) => void | Promise<void>

  // State serialization for persistence
  serializeState?: (panelId: string) => Record<string, JsonSerializable> | null
}

interface PanelLifecycleApi {
  panelId: string
  title: string
  setTitle(title: string): void
  close(): void
  focus(): void
  isActive: boolean
}

interface PaneProps {
  panelId: string
  params: Record<string, unknown>
  api: PanelLifecycleApi
  bridge: WorkspaceBridge
}

interface CommandConfig {
  id: string
  title: string
  run: () => void
  shortcut?: string
  when?: () => boolean
}
```

### getFileIcon utility (src/utils/fileIcons.ts)
- getFileIcon(filename: string): React.ComponentType<{ size: number }>
- Maps file extensions to lucide-react icons
- Used by FileTree and TabBar for consistent file icons
- Exported from public API: `export { getFileIcon } from './utils/fileIcons'`

### Panel authoring — typed object literals (no factory function)
```typescript
import type { PanelConfig } from '@boring/workspace'

export const csvViewer: PanelConfig = {
  id: 'csv-viewer',
  component: () => import('./CsvViewer'),
  title: 'CSV Viewer',
  icon: TableIcon,
  filePatterns: ['*.csv', '*.tsv'],
}

// Then in app shell:
<WorkspaceProvider panels={[csvViewer, imageViewer, agentPane]}>
```

Apps can override by registering a panel with more specific patterns:
```tsx
<WorkspaceProvider
  panels={[
    { id: 'csv-viewer', component: CsvViewer, filePatterns: ['*.csv', '*.tsv'] },
    { id: 'image-viewer', component: ImageViewer, filePatterns: ['*.png', '*.jpg', '*.svg'] },
  ]}
/>
// Now opening a .csv file routes to CsvViewer instead of code-editor
```

## v1 references
- panes.jsx (484 lines) — registration, lazy loading, capability gating
- DROP: requiresRouters, requiresFeatures (legacy)
- KEEP: requiresCapabilities for optional runtime gating

## Risk mitigations embedded
- **Risk 11** (Panel ID not found in registry): DockviewShell validates all panel IDs against the registry in onReady before calling api.addPanel(). Unknown ID → console.error with available IDs, skip the panel. In dev mode, throw to catch typos early.
- **Risk 13** (filePatterns routing precedence): Explicit precedence rules above. App panels always checked before built-ins. Longest suffix match wins. First registered breaks ties. Explicit panel param bypasses routing entirely.

## Considerations
- Panel registry populated by WorkspaceProvider from panels prop + built-in defaults
- Apps can override built-in panels by registering with same ID (app wins)
- App-registered panels with matching filePatterns override defaults
- Initial bundle = dockview + shadcn + zustand + registry only (~120KB gz). Every pane is a separate chunk.
- No definePanel() factory — typed object literal gives same autocomplete (ninth pass decision)

## Unit tests (registry/registry.test.ts)

### PanelRegistry
- Test: register(id, config) stores panel, get(id) returns it
- Test: register duplicate ID overwrites previous
- Test: list() returns all registered panels
- Test: has(id) returns true for registered, false for unregistered
- Test: getComponents() returns map of {id: React.lazy(component)} for all panels
- Test: getComponents() wraps sync components and async importers correctly

### File-type routing (resolve)
- Test: resolve('readme.md') → 'markdown-editor'
- Test: resolve('main.ts') → 'code-editor' (fallback)
- Test: resolve('data.csv') with csv-viewer registered → 'csv-viewer'
- Test: app panel *.ts overrides built-in *.ts (app checked first)
- Test: *.test.ts beats *.ts (longest suffix wins)
- Test: tied suffix length → first registered wins
- Test: resolve() with no matching panels → returns undefined/null

### Capability gating
- Test: panel with requiresCapabilities=['agent.chat'] + capabilities={'agent.chat': true} → available
- Test: panel with requiresCapabilities=['agent.chat'] + capabilities={} → filtered out
- Test: panel with no requiresCapabilities → always available
- Test: list() respects capability filter
- Test: getComponents() respects capability filter

### CommandRegistry
- Test: registerCommand(config) stores command, getCommand(id) returns it
- Test: getCommands() returns all registered commands
- Test: command with when: () => false filtered from active commands
- Test: built-in commands (Toggle Sidebar, Save File) auto-registered

### RegistryProvider
- Test: useRegistry() returns PanelRegistry inside RegistryProvider
- Test: useCommandRegistry() returns CommandRegistry inside RegistryProvider
- Test: hooks throw outside RegistryProvider context

### getFileIcon utility
- Test: getFileIcon('file.ts') returns TypeScript icon
- Test: getFileIcon('file.py') returns Python icon
- Test: getFileIcon('unknown.xyz') returns default file icon

## Success criteria
- register/get/list/has/resolve all work correctly
- Lazy loading works (React.lazy + Suspense)
- File-type routing respects precedence rules
- Capability gating filters unavailable panels
- CommandRegistry stores and retrieves commands
- Both registries accessible via hooks within WorkspaceProvider
- Built-in panels registered automatically
- App panels override built-ins when same ID provided
- getFileIcon returns correct icons for known extensions

title: Infra: Structured logging standard + breadcrumb pattern
description:
**Goal:** Every subsystem emits structured logs with a consistent prefix so CI failures are debuggable in one scroll. File: `src/server/logging.ts`.

**Pattern:**
```ts
export function createLogger(prefix: string) {
  return {
    info: (msg, fields?) => console.log(JSON.stringify({level:'info', prefix, msg, ...fields, t: new Date().toISOString()})),
    warn: ...,
    error: ...,
  }
}
```

**Required prefixes:**
- `[chat]` — chat route + stream
- `[sandbox:direct/bwrap/vercel]` — exec ops
- `[workspace]` — fs ops
- `[session]` — session CRUD
- `[bridge]` — UI bridge commands + state
- `[oidc]` — Vercel auth
- `[bake]` — snapshot bake
- `[ready-status]` — cold-start UX
- `[path-attack]` — rejected traversal attempts
- `[abort]` — abort signal flow
- `[plugin]` — pi extension loading
- `[test:<suite>]` — test suites (e2e, conformance, etc.)

**Log levels:** debug (verbose, gated by `BORING_AGENT_VERBOSE=1`), info, warn, error.

**Sensitive fields:** API keys, OIDC tokens → redacted (`***`) in all logs.

**Acceptance:** every subsystem file imports `createLogger(prefix)`; grep confirms no raw `console.log` in `src/server/**`.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every server file uses `createLogger(prefix)`. Grep confirms no raw console.log. Sensitive fields redacted.

title: M0: Interface — AgentTool + ToolCatalog + CatalogDeps
description:
**Goal:** Lock the catalog contract. File: `src/shared/tool.ts` + `src/shared/catalog.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
// tool.ts
export interface AgentTool<In = unknown, Out = unknown> {
  name: string
  description: string
  parameters: JsonSchema                // JSON Schema (AI SDK-native)
  execute(input: In, ctx: ToolCtx): Promise<Out>
}
export interface ToolCtx {
  abortSignal?: AbortSignal
  sessionId: string
  workspaceId: string
}

// catalog.ts
export interface CatalogDeps {
  workspace: Workspace
  sandbox: Sandbox
  uiBridge?: UiBridge                   // optional — tools that need it guard
  fileSearch?: FileSearch               // optional — tools that need it guard
}
export type ToolCatalog = (deps: CatalogDeps) => AgentTool[]
```

**Notes:**
- Parameters use **JSON Schema** (AI SDK-native). Pi uses TypeBox internally; we adapt at the harness boundary.
- `CatalogDeps.uiBridge` and `fileSearch` are **optional** — tools that need them guard internally (`if (!deps.uiBridge) return noop`). Document which built-in tools need which deps in the dep matrix.
- Name collisions: last-registered wins in v1 (documented in M4).

**Tests:** Type-level stub. Dep-matrix lint: ensure `standardCatalog` builds with only `{workspace, sandbox}` (uiBridge/fileSearch omitted → no crash, just degraded feature).

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/tool.ts` + `catalog.ts` export `AgentTool`, `ToolCatalog`, `CatalogDeps`. Parameters typed as JSON Schema. Type-level stub passes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/tool.ts` + `catalog.ts` export `AgentTool`, `ToolCatalog`, `CatalogDeps`. Parameters typed as JSON Schema. Type-level stub passes.

title: Phase 4: Command Palette + Keyboard Shortcuts
description:
## Goal
Cmd+P command palette (shadcn CommandDialog) + global keyboard shortcuts. Commands from CommandRegistry.

## What to implement

### Command Palette (src/components/CommandPalette.tsx)
- shadcn CommandDialog
- No prefix = file quick-open (fuzzy match from DataProvider file list)
- > prefix = filter registered commands from CommandRegistry
- Commands contributed via registry.registerCommand({ id, title, run, shortcut? })
- Built-in commands: Toggle Sidebar, Toggle Agent Panel, Save File, Close Tab

### Keyboard shortcuts
- Cmd+P → open command palette
- Cmd+B → toggle sidebar
- Cmd+\ → toggle agent panel
- Cmd+S → save active file
- Cmd+W → close active tab
- Workspace-level shortcuts on workspace container (always win via event.stopPropagation)
- Editor shortcuts (Cmd+Z undo, etc.) handled by focused editor only

### Integration
- CommandPalette rendered by WorkspaceProvider (always available)
- Panels and extensions register commands at runtime via CommandRegistry
- Shortcuts displayed in command palette entries

## v1 references
- useKeyboardShortcuts.js — shortcut registration pattern, priority handling

## Considerations
- Editor keybinding conflict (Risk 8): only focused editor captures keystrokes
- tiptap: setEditable(false) on blur, true on focus
- CM6: readOnly compartment reconfigured on blur/focus
- Workspace shortcuts always win (registered on container, not editors)

## Success criteria
- Cmd+P opens command palette
- File quick-open works with fuzzy matching
- > prefix shows registered commands
- All keyboard shortcuts work
- No keybinding conflicts between editors and workspace shortcuts

title: M0: Package scaffold (vite + fastify + tsup + vitest)
description:
**Goal:** Stand up the package build/dev infrastructure at `packages/agent/`. Most of this is done (package.json + tsconfig exist); this task finishes the wiring.

**Scope:**
- `packages/agent/package.json` — confirm exports map for `.` / `./shared` / `./server` / `./front`. Matches workspace plan's `import X from '@boring/agent'` expectation.
- `packages/agent/tsconfig.json` — strict, target ES2022, moduleResolution bundler, NodeNext compat for server.
- `tsup.config.ts` — build `dist/shared.js`, `dist/server.js`, `dist/front.js`, `dist/bin/boring-agent.js`.
- `vite.config.ts` — dev server for `app/` (standalone demo).
- `vitest.config.ts` — shared + server unit tests; jsdom for front-only.

- `pnpm test` runs vitest with zero tests (scaffold-clean).

**Done when:** Empty skeleton builds and tests pass on CI.

**Reference:** Old `packages/agent/package.json` for exports structure.

## Acceptance Criteria

`pnpm build` completes; `pnpm dev` boots empty Vite + Fastify; `pnpm test` runs vitest with zero tests. No behavior yet.
acceptance_criteria: `pnpm build` completes; `pnpm dev` boots empty Vite + Fastify; `pnpm test` runs vitest with zero tests. No behavior yet.

title: M3b: Slash commands infrastructure + 5 built-ins
description:
**Goal:** `/command` parser + registry + 5 built-ins. Files: `src/front/slashCommands/{parser,registry,builtins}.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Parser:** detects `^/(\w+)(\s+(.*))?$` in composer input; matches registered commands; falls through to normal message if no match.

**Registry API:**
```ts
export function useAgentChat() {
  ...
  return { ..., registerCommand(name, handler) }
}
// handler: (args: string) => void | Promise<void>
```

**5 built-ins:**
- `/clear` — hide (don't delete) messages from display.
- `/reset` — delete current session, create new (confirm).
- `/model <name>` — swap active model.
- `/help` — show command list.
- `/cost` — show per-session token/cost estimates (stubbed in v1: "coming soon" if no usage data yet).

**Tests:** each command fires correct action; unknown slash falls through as message.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 5 built-ins fire correct actions. Unknown slash falls through as message. registerCommand extension works.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 built-ins fire correct actions. Unknown slash falls through as message. registerCommand extension works.

title: M2: Snapshot-bake flow (one-time per python_packages hash)
description:
**Goal:** On backend startup, if `[sandbox].python_packages` is set and `snapshot_id` is not, create a seed sandbox, install packages, snapshot, cache the id. File: `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/bake.ts` (~150 LOC).

**Flow:**
1. Compute hash of sorted `python_packages + system_packages`.
2. Check local snapshot cache DB for matching hash → if found, use that snapshotId.
3. Otherwise: create seed sandbox with `runtime: python3.13`.
4. Install `system_packages` (dnf install …).
5. Install `python_packages` (`pip install`).
6. Call `sandbox.snapshot()`.
7. Cache `{hash, snapshotId}` locally (JSON file or via SandboxHandleStore extension).
8. Dispose seed sandbox.

**Triggers:** runs once at backend startup (async, non-blocking initial responses). If bake fails, log + fall back to per-call installs (slower but functional).

**Tests:** bake flow executes end-to-end against a mock Vercel client; hash stable across runs; existing cache skips bake.

## Acceptance Criteria

Bake executes end-to-end against mock. Hash stable across runs. Existing cache skips bake. Failure falls back to per-call installs with log.
acceptance_criteria: Bake executes end-to-end against mock. Hash stable across runs. Existing cache skips bake. Failure falls back to per-call installs with log.

title: M1: readTool factory
description:
**Goal:** `read` tool backed by `workspace.readFile`. File: `src/server/catalog/tools/readTool.ts`.

**Parameters:** `{ path: string, lineOffset?: number, lineCount?: number }` — supports partial reads for large files.

**Behavior:**
- Full read by default.
- `lineOffset + lineCount` returns a slice (1-indexed, matching Claude Code's read tool).
- Rejects paths escaping workspace root (handled by Workspace impl).
- Returns `{ content: string, totalLines: number, linesReturned: number }`.

**Tests:** full read; slice read; missing file rejects with clear error; traversal rejects.

## Acceptance Criteria

Full read + slice read both work. Missing file rejects clearly. Path traversal rejected at Workspace layer.
acceptance_criteria: Full read + slice read both work. Missing file rejects clearly. Path traversal rejected at Workspace layer.

title: Phase 1: WorkspaceProvider
description:
## Goal
Top-level provider component that composes all workspace contexts: registry, bridge, theme, data, store.

## What to implement

### WorkspaceProvider (src/WorkspaceProvider.tsx)
Composes 5 contexts into a single provider:
1. WorkspaceStoreContext — Zustand store (useWorkspaceStore)
2. RegistryContext — PanelRegistry + CommandRegistry (useRegistry, useCommandRegistry)
3. WorkspaceBridgeContext — bridge commands (useWorkspaceBridge)
4. ThemeContext — theme state (useTheme)
5. DataProviderContext — React Query hooks (useFileData, useFileContent, useFileList, useFileWrite)

### Full Props specification (WorkspaceProviderProps)

```typescript
interface WorkspaceProviderProps {
  children: React.ReactNode

  // --- Registry ---
  panels?: PanelConfig[]              // Additional panels beyond built-ins (e.g., ChatPanel)
                                       // Built-ins (filetree, editor, empty) auto-registered

  // --- Capabilities (for panel registry filtering) ---
  capabilities?: Record<string, boolean>  // e.g. { 'agent.chat': true, 'workspace.files': true }

  // --- Data layer ---
  apiBaseUrl?: string                 // Base URL for HTTP data provider. Default: '' (same origin)
  authHeaders?: Record<string, string> // Injected into every HTTP request (e.g., { Authorization: 'Bearer ...' })

  // --- Theme ---
  defaultTheme?: 'light' | 'dark'    // Initial theme. Default: 'light'
  onThemeChange?: (theme: 'light' | 'dark') => void

  // --- Persistence ---
  workspaceId?: string                // Scopes persistence key: 'boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId}'
                                       // Omit for single-workspace apps (falls back to 'boring-ui-v2:layout')
  storageKey?: string                 // Full override for persistence key (takes precedence over workspaceId)
  persistenceEnabled?: boolean        // Default: true. Set false for tests / ephemeral mode

  // --- Layout version migration (tenth pass) ---
  onLayoutVersionMismatch?: (persisted: string, current: string, layout: unknown) => SerializedLayout | null
                                       // Called when persisted layout version !== LayoutConfig.version
                                       // Return migrated layout or null (= reset to defaults)
                                       // Default: () => null (reset)

  // --- Bridge (Phase 3) ---
  bridgeEndpoint?: string             // SSE endpoint for agent→workspace commands. Default: '/api/v1/ui/commands/next'
                                       // Set null to disable bridge (standalone mode)

  // --- Error callbacks ---
  onLayoutError?: (error: Error) => void  // Invalid/corrupted layout detected
  onAuthError?: (statusCode: number) => void  // 401/403 from data provider
}
```

### Contexts provided (each accessible via hook)

| Context | Hook | What it provides |
|---------|------|-----------------|
| WorkspaceStoreContext | useWorkspaceStore() (internal) | Zustand store — layout, active panel, sidebar |
| WorkspaceBridgeContext | useWorkspaceBridge() | Imperative commands — openFile(), openPanel(), etc. |
| RegistryContext | useRegistry() | Panel registry — get(), list(), has(), getComponents() |
| ThemeContext | useTheme() | Theme state — { theme, setTheme } |
| DataProviderContext | useFileData() | React Query hooks — useFileContent(), useFileList(), useFileWrite() |

### Behavior
- Registers built-in panels + app panels into PanelRegistry
- Filters panels based on capabilities
- Sets up Zustand store with persistence configuration (workspaceId/storageKey scoping)
- Initializes bridge SSE connection (if bridgeEndpoint provided)
- Sets up theme provider with defaultTheme + persistence
- Sets up DataProvider with apiBaseUrl + authHeaders
- Sets up React Query QueryClient internally

### Risk 2 mitigation: Circular dependency (workspace ↔ agent)
Both types in core + component via props. Zero direct cross-imports.

- WorkspaceBridge interface + command types live in @boring/core. Workspace implements it, agent consumes the type from core.
- ChatPanel is NOT imported by workspace. Instead, workspace accepts a panels config prop. The app shell passes the agent component in:

```tsx
// In the app shell (NOT in workspace or agent package):
import { IdeLayout } from '@boring/workspace'
import { ChatPanel } from '@boring/agent'

<IdeLayout
  panels={[
    { id: 'agent', component: ChatPanel, placement: 'right' }
  ]}
/>
```

Workspace never imports from agent. Agent never imports from workspace. Both import types from core.

## Considerations
- Tier 3 (standalone) apps do NOT use WorkspaceProvider — components accept all data via props
- Tier 1/2 apps MUST wrap in WorkspaceProvider
- WorkspaceProvider is the ONLY component that knows about all the contexts
- Each context accessible via its own hook (no prop drilling)

## v1 references
- No direct single-provider equivalent in v1 (contexts scattered across App.jsx and layout components)
- v1 pattern: IdeLayout.jsx inlines many concerns that v2 extracts to WorkspaceProvider

## Unit tests (WorkspaceProvider.test.tsx)

### Context composition
- Test: all 5 contexts accessible via hooks inside WorkspaceProvider
- Test: useRegistry() returns PanelRegistry with built-in panels registered
- Test: useCommandRegistry() returns CommandRegistry with built-in commands
- Test: useWorkspaceBridge() returns WorkspaceBridge interface
- Test: useTheme() returns current theme
- Test: useFileData() hooks are available

### Panel registration
- Test: panels prop registers custom panels in registry alongside built-ins
- Test: custom panel with same ID as built-in overrides the built-in
- Test: capabilities prop filters unavailable panels from registry

### Persistence configuration
- Test: workspaceId sets correct localStorage key (boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId})
- Test: storageKey overrides workspaceId when both provided
- Test: persistenceEnabled=false → no localStorage reads/writes

### Bridge initialization
- Test: bridgeEndpoint provided → SSE connection initiated
- Test: bridgeEndpoint=null → no SSE connection (standalone mode)
- Test: bridgeEndpoint not provided → uses default '/api/v1/ui/commands/next'

### Error callbacks
- Test: onAuthError called on 401 from data provider
- Test: onLayoutError called on corrupted layout detection

### Theme
- Test: defaultTheme='dark' → initial theme is dark
- Test: onThemeChange callback fires when theme changes

## Success criteria
- All 5 contexts accessible via hooks inside WorkspaceProvider
- panels prop registers custom panels in registry
- capabilities prop filters unavailable panels
- Persistence configured with correct workspaceId-scoped key
- Bridge connection established when bridgeEndpoint provided
- Works with IdeLayout and ChatLayout children
- Type-safe props with full TypeScript autocomplete
